description.p: As an action for 2 CE, you can cast Levitate. You can spend an additional 2 CE to force the creature to make the save at disadvantage.

sheet.description: For 2 CE, you can cast Levitate. You can spend an additional 2 CE to force the creature to make the save at disadvantage.

description.p: For an additional 3 CE, your Antigravity Shield instead reduces all damage against you by half your Antigravity's maximum height fall damage dice until the beginning of your next turn.

sheet.description: For an additional 3 CE, your Antigravity Shield instead reduces all damage against you by half your Antigravity's maximum height fall damage dice until the beginning of your next turn.

description.p: As an action for 3 CE, you can give yourself or another creature you touch a 60 feet hover speed for 10 minutes.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, you can give yourself or another creature you touch a 60 feet hover speed for 10 minutes.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Auspicious Beast #1: Kaichi

description.p: Your proficiency with your technique and bond with Kaichi improves. You can summon up to two Kaichi horns at the same time as part of your action. This lets you make an additional attack with your horns.

sheet.description: You can summon up to two Kaichi horns at the same time as part of your action. This lets you make an additional attack with your horns.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Auspicious Beast #2: Reiki

description.p: Your proficiency with your technique and bond with Reiki improves. While your movement speed is increased by Reiki, you gain a +3 bonus to AC and don't provoke opportunity attacks.

sheet.description: While your movement speed is increased by Reiki, you gain a +3 bonus to AC and don't provoke opportunity attacks.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Auspicious Beast #3: Kirin

description.p: Your proficiency with your technique reaches a crescendo, and your bond with Kirin improves. Whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack while you have Kirin activated, you can force them to take fire damage equal to twice the damage taken.

sheet.description: Whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack while you have Kirin activated, you can force them to take fire damage equal to twice the damage taken.

description.p: You have improved your ability to manage information. While you see through the crows you don't gain the blinded condition.

sheet.description: While you see through the crows you don't gain the blinded condition.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Omen of Death

description.p: You have learned from the opportunistic way of the crows. After attacking a frightened creature you gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

sheet.description: After attacking a frightened creature you gain {{proficiency}} temporary hit points.

description.p: Through trial and error, your mastery over Bird Strike has reached new heights. Whenever you use Bird Strike you can choose between either increasing the damage die to d12 instead of d10, or to roll an additional d6 to damage a limb.

sheet.description: Whenever you use Bird Strike you can choose between either increasing the damage die to d12 instead of d10, or to roll an additional d6 to damage a limb.

description.p: Once per turn, you can choose to deal additional fire damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

sheet.description: Once per turn, you can choose to deal {{proficiency}} additional fire damage.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Mantle of Courage

description.p: When you use Mantle of Courage, all creatures of your choice in range gain 5 temporary hit points and again at the start of your turn for the duration.

sheet.description: When you use Mantle of Courage, all creatures of your choice in range gain 5 temporary hit points and again at the start of your turn for the duration.

description.p: You have learned to burn even the soul. When you hit with a melee attack, you can spend 3 CE to cause all fire damage dealt on your turn to ignore immunity to fire damage and reduce a creature's maximum hit points until they complete a long rest.

sheet.description: When you hit with a melee attack, you can spend 3 CE to cause all fire damage dealt on your turn to ignore immunity to fire damage and reduce a creature's maximum hit points until they complete a long rest.

description.p: When you hit with an attack while Ignition is activated, you can release the technique, ending the effects of Ignition on the weapon in exchange for doubling your fire damage for this attack. If used with Maximum: Conflagration, it instead deals a number of additional damage die equal to the CE spent on Ignition.

sheet.description: When you hit with an attack while Ignition is activated you can end the effects of Ignition on the weapon in exchange for doubling your fire damage for this attack. If used with Maximum: Conflagration, it instead deals a number of additional damage die equal to the CE spent on Ignition.

description.p: You have found a way to link your blood and Cursed Energy supply. For 1 CE, you can create a sufficient source of blood for 10 HP.

sheet.description: For 1 CE, you can create a sufficient source of blood for 10 HP.

description.p: Your blood has grown to be harmful to others. If you hit a creature by 5 or more than their AC or a creature fails a save against a Blood Manipulation technique by 5 or more, the target is poisoned for 1 minute. They can make a Constitution save at the end of their turn to cure the condition.

sheet.description: If you hit a creature by 5 or more than their AC or a creature fails a save against a Blood Manipulation technique by 5 or more, the target is poisoned for 1 minute. They can make a Constitution save at the end of their turn to cure the condition.

description.p: You gain the ability to harden your blood to a solid, even if it risks thrombosis and further increases your versatility. As a bonus action for 3 CE. you gain the following for 1 minute:

ul.li: As a reaction, you can add a +5 to your AC against an attack.
Your unarmed damage dice increases by two levels (d8-d10-d12-2d8)
You gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

description.p: At the end of the duration, you take 2d10 necrotic damage as your blood returns to its normal state.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, for 1 minute you can as a reaction add +5 to your AC against an attack, your unarmed damage dice increases by two levels, and you gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. At the end of the duration, you take 2d10 necrotic damage.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Flowing Red Scale

description.p: The power of the Flowing Red Scale has reached new heights. As a bonus action for 6 CE or while Flowing Red Scale is active for 3 CE, you instead gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

ul.li: Your movement speed is tripled.
You gain a +4 bonus to AC.
You have advantage on Dexterity and Strength saving throws and Strength checks and once per round you can reroll a check or save.
You gain one extra action and reaction.

sheet.description: For 6 CE or while Flowing Red Scale is active for 3 CE, you instead gain the following benefits for 1 minute: Your movement speed is tripled, you gain a +4 bonus to AC, you have advantage on Dexterity and Strength saving throws and Strength checks, and you gain one extra action and reaction.

description.p: You can create small orbs of condensed blood that explode like a buckshot. As an action, you condense your Piercing Blood into a tiny orb of blood in an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet, which stays there for a maximum of 1 minute before exploding. You can create as many orbs as your proficiency bonus in an action with each having its own cost, spending 5 CE and 5 HP for each, and can choose for them to explode immediately upon creation or manually as a reaction to a creature entering the range of explosion.

The orb then explodes, forcing every creature within 10 feet of it to make a Dexterity saving throw for each orb. On a failure, the target takes 8d8 piercing damage and is knocked prone. On a success, they take half as much damage and aren't knocked prone.

sheet.description: For 5 CE and 5 HP, create up to {{proficiency}} orbs of blood, spending CE and HP for each one, in an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet which stays there for a maximum of 1 minute before exploding. You can choose for them to explode immediately or as a reaction to a creature entering the range of explosion. When a orb explodes each creature within 10 feet of it makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 8d8 piercing damage and is knocked prone. On a success, they take half as much damage and aren't knocked prone.

description.p: You condense your blood into wings resembling veins as a bonus action for 10 CE, gathering the wings behind you and gaining the following benefits for 1 minute:

ul.li: Your walking speed is quadrupled.
You gain a flying speed equal to half your walking speed.

As a free action on your turn, you can unleash lines of blood. This takes shape as three ranged Cursed Energy attacks against a creature within 60 feet. Each attack deals 3d10 + your CE modifier in necrotic damage.

description.p: While using Wing King, you cannot use Piercing Blood.

sheet.description: For 10 CE, for 1 minute your walking speed is quadrupled, you gain a {{speed}}/2 flying speed, and as a free action on your turn make 3 ranged CE attacks against a creature within 60 feet. Each attack deals 3d10 + {{ce:modifier}} necrotic damage. While using Wing King, you cannot use Piercing Blood.

description.p: Your Boogie has increased in speed and the unpredictability of your Boogie. You gain 2 additional reactions which can only be used to Boogie. Additionally, when you Boogie the next attack made against you has disadvantage.

sheet.description: You gain 2 additional reactions which can only be used to Boogie. When you Boogie the next attack made against you has disadvantage.

description.p: The tricky timing and misdirection with Woogie has improved. After getting swapped, the enemy creature must make an Intelligence save or be stunned until the end of their next turn. On a success, they gain advantage on future saves. Additionally, as an action you can fake clap, forcing them to make an Insight check versus your Deception or Performance. On a failure, they waste their next attack by attacking in the wrong direction.

sheet.description: After getting swapped, enemy creatures must make an Intelligence save or be stunned until the end of their next turn. On a success, they gain advantage on future saves. As an action you can fake clap, forcing them to make an Insight check versus your Deception or Performance. On a failure, they waste their next attack by attacking in the wrong direction.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Round of Applause

description.p: Boogie Woogie, as effective as it is in solo combat, only reaches its full potential not with an ally, but with a best friend. At the start of combat, you can choose one ally as a Best Friend. When fighting with that ally, you gain the following benefits:

ul.li: When you switch the place of your Best Friend with an ally who was previously in melee range of an enemy, on top of advantage, the attack will have its critical range increased by 3.
Swapping your Best Friend has no cost.
When flanking with your Best Friend, you and your Best Friend gain a bonus to damage rolls equal to your proficiency bonus.

sheet.description: At the start of combat, you can choose one ally as a Best Friend. When fighting with that ally, when you switch the place of your Best Friend with an ally who was previously in melee range of an enemy, the next attack will have its critical range increased by 3, swapping your Best Friend has no cost, and when flanking with your Best Friend, you and your Best Friend gain a + {{proficiency}} bonus to damage rolls.

description.p: Your acting skills from hopping bodies transfer to other uses. You must spend seven days and 25 gp to establish the history, profession, and affiliations for an identity. You can't establish an identity that belongs to someone else. For example, you might acquire appropriate clothing, letters of introduction, and official-looking certification to establish yourself as a member of a trading house from a remote city so you can insinuate yourself into the company of others.

sheet.description: You must spend seven days and 25 gp to establish the history, profession, and affiliations for an identity. You can't establish an identity that belongs to someone else.

description.p: Memories fade and wane, you however have grown beyond that. You remember everything you experience, and you can store them in objects allowing anyone who touches them to experience them from your point of view.

sheet.description: You remember everything you experience, and you can store them in objects allowing anyone who touches them to experience them from your point of view.

description.p: As much as you might as well be immortal, it never hurts to be prepared. As an action, after having a dead body prepared, you can prepare it as a backup. This backup works the same as the Clone spell, without the cost or time requirement.

sheet.description: After having a dead body prepared, you can prepare it as a backup. This backup works the same as the Clone spell, without the cost or time requirement.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have One Man Army

description.p: As a reaction to damage, you can spend 4 CE to summon a Clone, which shoves you 5 feet and takes the damage in your place.

sheet.description: As a reaction to damage, you can spend 4 CE to summon a Clone, which shoves you 5 feet and takes the damage in your place.

element.name: Unstoppable Legion
element.prerequisite: Must have Three is a Crowd

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Three is a Crowd

description.p: You have trained extensively in fighting together with your Clones, gaining the following benefits:

ul.li: When flanking with a Clone, you deal additional damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
You can create Clones as a bonus action for 1 extra CE per clone.
When one of your Clones is reduced to 0 hit points, rather than waiting 1 minute, you only need to wait 1 round.

sheet.description: When flanking with a Clone, you deal {{proficiency}} additional damage. You can create Clones as a bonus action for 1 extra CE per clone. When one of your Clones is reduced to 0 hit points, rather than waiting 1 minute, you only need to wait 1 round.

element.name: Maximum: Conga Line
element.prerequisite: Must have Four is a Party

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Four is a Party

description.p: You can spend 8 CE as a free action to tag team an enemy with your Clones, making a Conga Line on your way to stomp them. You and all clones can immediately move 30 feet and take the attack action.

sheet.description: You can spend 8 CE as a free action to allow you and all clones to immediately move 30 feet and take the attack action.

description.p: Even when you're the one telling the joke, you can't stop laughing. For an additional 2 CE when you activate Comedy, you can make the technique last 10 minutes. This does not work with Peak Comedy.

sheet.description: For an additional 2 CE when you activate Comedy, you can make the technique last 10 minutes. This does not work with Peak Comedy.

element.name: Super Gag
element.prerequisite: Must have Commit to the Bit

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Commit to the Bit

description.p: You have stepped up your game with some premium material you read online. You gain the following gags while your technique is active:

ul.li: Written on your Face. As an action for 3 CE, you target a creature within 30 ft that you can see. For 1 minute or when your technique ends, the target's emotions and intentions are literally written on their face. The target has disadvantage on attacks against anyone who can see their face. Anyone that can see their face knows their general emotions and can tell whenever they are scheming or planning something by reading the doodles on their face.

Costume Switch. As an action for 2 CE, you apply a wardrobe change to a number of creatures equal to your CE modifier within 30 ft of you that you can see. This effect is purely cosmetic and cannot change their AC or movement and cannot give them any conditions. You can also change a property about them, such as giving them multiple eyes or arms. These changes are nonfunctional and a creature can take an action to make a Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) against your CE save DC inspect these changes. On a success, they realize the changes are fake. This feature lasts for a number of minutes equal to your CE modifier before disappearing.

sheet.description: While your technique is active, you can use Written on your Face and Costume Switch.

description.p: As an action for 3 CE, you target a creature within 30 ft that you can see. For 1 minute or when your technique ends, the target's emotions and intentions are literally written on their face. The target has disadvantage on attacks against anyone who can see their face. Anyone that can see their face knows their general emotions and can tell whenever they are scheming or planning something by reading the doodles on their face.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, target a creature within 30 ft that you can see. For 1 minute or when your technique ends, the target has disadvantage on attacks against anyone who can see their face. Anyone that can see their face knows their general emotions and can tell whenever they are scheming or planning something.

description.p: As an action for 2 CE, you apply a wardrobe change to a number of creatures equal to your CE modifier within 30 ft of you that you can see. This effect is purely cosmetic and cannot change their AC or movement and cannot give them any conditions. You can also change a property about them, such as giving them multiple eyes or arms. These changes are nonfunctional and a creature can take an action to make a Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) against your CE save DC inspect these changes. On a success, they realize the changes are fake. This feature lasts for a number of minutes equal to your CE modifier before disappearing.

sheet.description: For 2 CE, apply a wardrobe change to up to {{ce:modifier}} creatures within 30 ft of you that you can see. This effect is purely cosmetic. These changes are nonfunctional and a creature can take an action to make a Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) against your CE save DC to inspect these changes. On a success, they realize the changes are fake. This feature lasts for {{ce:modifier}} minutes.

description.p: As a bonus action for 3 CE while your technique is active, you summon an invisible audience around you. For the next minute, creatures within 30 ft have disadvantage on saves against your techniques and you have advantage on ability checks and saves to maintain your technique.

sheet.description: For 3 CE while your technique is active, for the next minute, creatures within 30 ft have disadvantage on saves against your techniques and you have advantage on ability checks and saves to maintain your technique.

description.p: After creating an item, you can make a Sleight of Hand check against a creature's passive Perception. On a success, your first attack or check using this creation has advantage.

sheet.description: After creating an item, you can attempt a Sleight of Hand check vs passive Perception to gain advantage on your first attack or check with it.

description.p: You have broken down your ability to create a cursed tool into its very essence, including imbuing cursed techniques into objects. At twice the cost of the normal creation of a Cursed Tool, you can imbue an existing item with that cursed tool's Technique for one week. At 6th level this becomes 2 weeks, 3 weeks at 11th level, and a month at 18th level.

sheet.description: At twice the cost of the normal creation of a Cursed Tool, you can imbue an existing item with that cursed tool's Technique for one week.
At twice the cost of the normal creation of a Cursed Tool, you can imbue an existing item with that cursed tool's Technique for two weeks.
At twice the cost of the normal creation of a Cursed Tool, you can imbue an existing item with that cursed tool's Technique for three weeks.
At twice the cost of the normal creation of a Cursed Tool, you can imbue an existing item with that cursed tool's Technique for one month.

description.p: Through research and creativity, you have created a Perfect Sphere with no contact area which generates infinite pressure. As an action for 10 Cursed Energy, you create a 10-foot diameter perfect sphere of metal that appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of you and lasts for 1 minute while concentrating. Any creature that touches or ends its turn within 5 feet of the sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw. The creature takes 8d12 force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

As a bonus action, you can move the sphere up to 30 feet. If you ram the sphere into a creature, that creature must make the saving throw against the sphere's damage, and the sphere stops moving this turn. When you move the sphere, it destroys all barriers and non-magical structures in its way.

sheet.description: For 10 Cursed Energy, create a 10-foot diameter sphere that appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of you and lasts for 1 minute while concentrating. Any creature that touches or ends its turn within 5 feet of the sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw. The creature takes 8d12 force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. As a bonus action, you can move the sphere up to 30 feet. If you ram the sphere into a creature, that creature must make the saving throw against the sphere's damage, and the sphere stops moving this turn. When you move the sphere, it destroys all barriers and non-magical structures in its way.

description.p: As a reaction against any hostile action taken against you, you can materialize one of your receipts for twice the cursed energy cost in a surprise attack. If using an object, you force the creature to make a Dexterity saving throw at disadvantage if they are within range. On a failure, they take bludgeoning damage depending on the object's size. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage. If using a weapon, you can make an attack with the weapon against the attacker at advantage if they are within range. At 11th level, this can be used with Contractual Control with only the one Object or Weapon used as part of this reaction.

sheet.description: As a reaction against any hostile action taken against you, you can attack with a receipt for twice the CE cost. If using an object, you force the creature to make a Dexterity saving throw at disadvantage if they are within range. On a failure, they take bludgeoning damage depending on the object's size. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage. If using a weapon, you can make an attack with the weapon against the attacker at advantage if they are within range.

description.p: You've learned that buying in bulk can lead to a sale! When you use two or more receipts as part of the same action, the CE costs are each reduced by 1 for every receipt you use (minimum cost of 1). If the receipts are of the same type, you gain advantage on attack rolls and ability checks using those creations for 1 minute.

sheet.description: When you use two or more receipts as part of the same action, the CE costs are each reduced by 1 for every receipt you use (minimum cost of 1). If the receipts are of the same type, you gain advantage on attack rolls and ability checks using those creations for 1 minute.

description.p: You have become a master at utilizing your receipts. The cost of materializing is halved, and creations you use with Contractual Control can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus before they disappear.

sheet.description: The cost of materialization is halved. Your creations are usable {{proficiency}} times.

description.p: You have realized a new way to utilize your massive output. You can now use your Granite Blast as a melee cursed energy attack. This cannot be used with Grape Shot.

sheet.description: Granite Blast can be used as a melee CE attack. This cannot be used with Grape Shot.

description.p: The ability to control your Granite Blasts has improved further. Once per turn on a hit with one Granite Blast, you can spend an additional 2 Cursed Energy, forcing all creatures within 5 feet of the target to make a Dexterity save, taking half the damage on a failed save as the attack splits on impact.

sheet.description: Once per turn on a hit with one Granite Blast, you can spend an additional 2 CE, forcing all creatures within 5 feet of the target to make a Dexterity save, taking half the damage on a failed save.

description.p: You have found a brand new and destructive pathway for your technique. When you use Nuclear Shell, you can instead spend 6 CE to change the area of effect to a 100-foot radius sphere surrounding yourself. On a failure, all creatures are knocked prone and stunned until the beginning of their next turn. All structures take maximum damage and non magical objects not worn or held are reduced to dust.

sheet.description: When you use Nuclear Shell, you can instead spend 6 CE to change the area of effect to a 100-foot radius sphere surrounding yourself. On a failure, all creatures are knocked prone and stunned until the beginning of their next turn. All structures take maximum damage and non magical objects not worn or held are reduced to dust.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Cursed Corpse Creation

description.p: You have spent your life learning your technique and learned tips and tricks that help you master the process. You can create a cursed core or body for 1/3 of the cost or time.

sheet.description: You can create a cursed core or body for 1/3 of the cost or time.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Cursed Corpse Creation

description.p: You have learned to copy a creature's soul information into a Cursed Corpse. You can only have 1 soul imbued corpse at a time. This increases to 2 at 11th level and 3 at 18th level.

It takes 3 long rests to imbue someone's soul information into the core. You must have the person readily available or have a good memory of the person to turn their physical information into soul information. Once a corpse has been created, it takes 2 weeks to develop before it becomes sentient. It gains the Autonomy mutation and can choose a Cursed Energy feat as if it were a player. A corpse with this core is sentient and mimics the behavior of the soul they are imbued with, but it does not gain its memories.

sheet.description: You can have {{corpse:creation:soul:imbuement:max}} soul imbued corpse at a time. It takes 3 long rests to imbue someone's soul information into the core. You must have the person readily available or have a good memory of the person to turn their physical information into soul information. Once a corpse has been created, it takes 2 weeks to develop before it becomes sentient. It gains the Autonomy mutation and can choose a Cursed Energy feat as if it were a player. A corpse with this core is sentient and mimics the behavior of the soul they are imbued with, but it does not gain its memories.

description.p: When you use Corpse Tactics, you can spend CE up to your proficiency bonus to use an additional tactics option for each CE spent. You can choose the same tactics option as part of the same bonus action, but you cannot use the same tactics multiple times on the same cursed corpse.

sheet.description: When you use Corpse Tactics, you can spend up to {{proficiency}} CE to use an additional tactic per CE spent. You can choose the same tactics option, but cannot use the same tactics multiple times on the same cursed corpse.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Cursed Corpse Mutation

description.p: You have learned the process to purposely force mutations to occur. When you roll for a mutation, you can add your Cursed Energy modifier to the roll. If you succeed, you roll for an additional mutation for every number higher than 20. You cannot gain the same mutation more than once.

sheet.description: When you roll for a mutation, you can add {{ce:modifier}} to the roll. If you succeed, you roll for an additional mutation for every number higher than 20. You cannot gain the same mutation more than once.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Power of Words

description.p: “Oh, them's fighting words.” You have found a way to make your words insulting. When using a word, you can force the enemy to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they have disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you.

sheet.description: When you use a word, you can force a Wisdom save. On a failure, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you.

description.p: Your versatility and training have increased the amount of words you have and your speed. You can choose one more word to be under the effect of Interjection.

You have found a way to increase your power using an outside tool. For 3 Cursed Energy you can create or imbue a megaphone for 1 minute that causes your words to affect a 60-foot cone. The megaphone has hit points equal to twice your CE score and damages the megaphone instead of your vocal chords when used in this way. When reduced to 0 hit points, the megaphone turns to dust.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, create a megaphone for 1 minute that makes your words affect a 60-foot cone. The megaphone has {{cursed:speech:megaphone:hp}} HP and takes damage instead of your vocal chords. It is destroyed at 0 HP.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Curse Infused Spirits

description.p: You have learned to weaponize the affinity cursed spirits have for regeneration. Once per turn, as an action, bonus action, or reaction, you can spend up to your cursed energy modifier to restore 5 hit points per cursed energy spent to a cursed spirit you have summoned within 60 feet of you.
At 11th level, this becomes 10 hit points per cursed energy spent.

sheet.description: Once per turn, spend up to {{ce:modifier}} CE to heal a summoned cursed spirit within 60 ft for 5 HP per CE .
Once per turn, spend up to {{ce:modifier}} CE to heal a summoned cursed spirit within 60 ft for 10 HP per CE .

description.p: Your reaction speed and control over your cursed spirits has increased. As a reaction, for 5 Cursed Energy, you can summon a CR 5 or lower cursed spirit that takes as much of the triggering damage as it can. You take any remaining damage. If the spirit survives, it appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you.

sheet.description: For 5 CE, summon a CR 5 or lower cursed spirit that absorbs incoming damage. You take any remaining damage. If it survives, it appears within an unoccupied space within 5 ft of you.

description.p: Due to your extended studies, you have made a smaller version of Uzumaki that's more deadly and much harder to dodge. As a bonus action, you can spend two cursed spirits to create a miniature Uzumaki that fits in your hand. It can't be seen by the creature unless its passive Perception is 25 or higher, or the creature succeeds a Perception check. As an action or reaction for 5 Cursed Energy, you can release all the condensed energy from this Uzumaki at a target within 30 feet. Make a ranged cursed energy attack roll, on a hit the target takes Xd12 of necrotic damage(X being the combined CR of the 2 curses). Like Maximum Uzumaki, this ignores resistance and treats immunity like resistance. You have advantage on your attack roll if the target has not detected your Minimum Uzumaki.
Similar to Maximum Uzumaki, you can obtain actions, bonus actions, reactions, or legendary actions tied to the absorbed technique.

sheet.description: Spend 2 cursed spirits to create a miniature Uzumaki that can't be seen by creatures unless they have at least a 25 passive Perception or suceed on a Perception check. As an action or reaction for 5 CE, make a ranged CE attack at a target within 30 feet. On a hit they take Xd12 necrotic (X = combined CR of cursed spirits). This ignores resistance, treats immunity as resistance, and you have advantage if undetected. You can extract the features of cursed spirits used this way.

description.p: When you hit a creature with a claw attack while within 5 feet, you can attempt to grapple the creature as a bonus action. While grappling a creature, it takes your claw damage at the start of its turn.

sheet.description: On a claw hit within 5 ft, you can attempt to grapple the target. Grappled creatures take your claw damage at the start of their turn.

description.p: You have learned to control the length of your claws. As a bonus action, you lengthen your claws and increase your melee reach with them by 5 ft for 1 minute or until you retract them as a bonus action.

Additionally, you learn to shoot out your claws as projectiles. Once per turn when you take the Attack action you can spend 1 CE to replace one of your attacks with a Drill Claw attack. You make a ranged Claw attack using your Strength, Dexterity, or Cursed Energy modifier against a creature within 30 ft. On a hit, this attack deals 4d8 piercing damage. This increases to 6d8 at 6th level, 8d8 at 11th level and 10d8 at 18th level. The range of this attack doubles at 6th level.

sheet.description: Increase your melee range with your claws by 5 ft for 1 minute or until retracted as a bonus action. Once per turn when you take the Attack action you can spend 1 CE to replace one of your attacks with a ranged Claw attack using your Strength, Dexterity, or CE modifier against a creature within {{dark:claw:drill:claws:range}} ft. On a hit, this attack deals {{dark:claw:drill:claws:damage}}d8 piercing damage.

description.p: You have learned to sharpen your claws with cursed energy. You gain a +1 bonus to critical range with your Claws and can reroll and 1s or 2s when rolling damage for your Claws. The bonus to critical range increases to +2 at 11th level and +3 at 18th level.

sheet.description: Your claws have a +{{dark:claw:cutting:edge:range}} to their critical range and can reroll 1s and 2s on damage rolls.

description.p: Through a broadening of your understanding over your gavel. On top of your normal forms, you can turn your Gavel into any melee weapon. You also gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls using your Gavel. This increases to a +2 bonus at 6th level and +3 bonus at 11th level. The Executioner's Sword does not gain this bonus.

sheet.description: Your Gavel can become any melee weapon and gains a +{{deadly:sentencing:tool:versatility:bonus}} to attack and damage rolls. The Executioner's Sword does not gain this bonus.

element.name: Weight of Judgment
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Deadly Sentencing

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Deadly Sentencing

description.p: When you hit a creature with your Judge's Gavel, you can spend 2 CE to double the weapon's damage dice. The creature must also make a Strength saving throw or fall prone.

sheet.description: On a hit with your Gavel for 2 CE, double the weapon's damage dice and force the target to succeed on a Strength save or fall prone.

description.p: Your attunement with Deadly Sentencing has allowed you to summon Judgeman outside the domain. As a bonus action for 5 Cursed Energy, you summon Judgeman for 1 minute. Every time your opponent attacks you, they are penalized for assault and the opponent gains a -1 penalty to attack rolls and saving throws. If a creature targets you with a cursed technique that is not an attack roll, they gain a -1 penalty to their CE save DC. These penalties last until Judgeman disappears.

sheet.description: For 5 CE, for 1 minute every time a opponent attacks you they gain a -1 penalty to attack rolls and saving throws. If a creature targets you with a cursed technique that is not an attack roll, they gain a -1 penalty to their CE save DC. These penalties last for the duration.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Flames of Disaster

description.p: Through training and understanding, you have found new applications to your flames. You gain the one of the following (This feat can be taken more than once):

ul.li: Resistance to Fire Damage and when you roll damage for a technique that deals fire damage, you can reroll any 1s, but must take the new result, even if the new roll is a 1.
Two spells below 5th level dealing fire damage. The ce cost equals the spell's level.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Flames of Disaster

description.p: Through training and understanding, you have found new applications to your flames. You gain the one of the following (This feat can be taken more than once):

ul.li: Resistance to Fire Damage and when you roll damage for a technique that deals fire damage, you can reroll any 1s, but must take the new result, even if the new roll is a 1.
Two spells below 5th level dealing fire damage. The CE cost equals the spell's level.

description.p: Resistance to Fire Damage and when you roll damage for a technique that deals fire damage, you can reroll any 1s, but must take the new result, even if the new roll is a 1.

sheet.description: When you roll damage for a technique that deals fire damage, you can reroll any 1s, but must take the new result.

description.p: Two spells below 5th level dealing fire damage. The CE cost equals the spell's level.

sheet.description: You can cast the selected spells for CE equal to the spell's level.

description.p: Through focus and thorough planning, you have gained further ideas. As a bonus action, you can gain a 60 foot hover speed for as many rounds as Cursed Energy spent.

sheet.description: Spend CE to gain a 60 ft hover speed for that many rounds.

description.p: Your presence can now be felt, all around. As an action for 10 Cursed Energy, you can cause all non magical objects and structures of your choice in a 100 foot radius to melt. As an action you can control where the lava flows or control it directly to make two cursed energy attacks against a creature within range, dealing 10d8 fire damage on a hit. Each creature that enters the lava or starts their turn touching the lava must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

sheet.description: For 10 CE, all non magical objects and structures of your choice in a 100 foot radius melt. As an action you can control the lava to make two CE attacks against a creature within range, dealing 10d8 fire damage on a hit. Each creature that enters the lava or starts their turn touching the lava must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Tides of Disaster

description.p: Through training and understanding, you have found new applications to your water. You gain the one of the following (You can select this feat multiple times):

ul.li: Piercing Water: You make a small water beam made to pierce through enemies. As an action for 2 CE, you make a cursed energy attack against a creature within 30 ft. On a hit, they take 2d10 cold damage + 2d6 piercing damage and are forced to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked prone and will receive the damage rolled one additional time as a vital organ is hit. On a success, they don't take additional damage and are not prone.
Two water spells below 5th level. The CE cost equals the spell's level in Cursed Energy.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Tides of Disaster

description.p: Through training and understanding, you have found new applications to your water. You gain the one of the following (You can select this feat multiple times):

ul.li: Piercing Water: You make a small water beam made to pierce through enemies. As an action for 2 CE, you make a cursed energy attack against a creature within 30 ft. On a hit, they take 2d10 cold damage + 2d6 piercing damage and are forced to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked prone and will receive the damage rolled one additional time as a vital organ is hit. On a success, they don't take additional damage and are not prone.
Two water spells below 5th level. The CE cost equals the spell's level in Cursed Energy.

description.p: You make a small water beam made to pierce through enemies. As an action for 2 CE, you make a cursed energy attack against a creature within 30 ft. On a hit, they take 2d10 cold damage + 2d6 piercing damage and are forced to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked prone and will receive the damage rolled one additional time as a vital organ is hit. On a success, they don't take additional damage and are not prone.

sheet.description: For 2 CE, make a cursed energy attack against a creature within 30 ft. On a hit, they take 2d10 cold damage + 2d6 piercing damage and are forced to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are knocked prone and will receive the damage rolled one additional time. On a success, they don't take additional damage and are not prone.

description.p: Two water spells below 5th level. The CE cost equals the spell's level in Cursed Energy.

sheet.description: You can cast the selected spells for CE equal to the spell's level.

element.name: Shoal
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Horizon of Captivating Skhanda

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Horizon of Captivating Skhanda

description.p: You have increased your technique's potential. You can now use Death Swarm outside your domain without the sure hit effect. You can have a number of shikigami equal to your Cursed Energy modifier when used in this way. The number of shikigami increases by your CE modifier at 6th level (2x), 11th level (3x), and 18th level (4x).

sheet.description: You can use Death Swarm outside your domain without the sure hit. You can have up to {{disaster:tides:shoal:max}} shikigami when used this way.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Water Defensive Ring

description.p: The scale and power of the water ring surrounding you has increased. For an extra 4 cursed energy, it also creates a 20 ft radius sphere of whirling storm centered on you. Each creature other than you in the sphere when it appears or that ends its turn there must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The sphere's space is difficult terrain.

Until the technique ends, you can use a bonus action to cause a bolt of lightning to leap from you toward a creature within 60 feet, making a cursed energy attack. You have advantage on the attack if the target is in the sphere. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 lightning damage. Creatures within 30 feet of the sphere have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks to hear.

sheet.description: For an extra 4 CE, Water Defensive Ring also creates a 20 ft radius sphere centered on you. Each other creature in the sphere when it appears or that ends its turn there must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The sphere's space is difficult terrain. Until the technique ends, you can use a bonus action to make a CE attack at a creature within 60 feet. You have advantage on the attack if the target is in the sphere. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 lightning damage. Creatures within 30 feet of the sphere have disadvantage on Perception checks to hear.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Forest of Disaster

description.p: Through training and understanding, you have found new applications to your roots. You gain the one of the following (You can select this feat multiple times):

ul.li: You can transform your root wall into any of the other forms as a bonus action.
Two spells below 5th level dealing with nature. The CE cost equals the spell's level.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Forest of Disaster

description.p: Through training and understanding, you have found new applications to your roots. You gain the one of the following (You can select this feat multiple times):

ul.li: You can transform your root wall into any of the other forms as a bonus action.
Two spells below 5th level dealing with nature. The CE cost equals the spell's level.

description.p: You can transform your root wall into any of the other forms as a bonus action.

sheet.description: Transform your root wall into any of the other forms

description.p: Two spells below 5th level dealing with nature. The CE cost equals the spell's level.

sheet.description: You can cast the selected spells for CE equal to the spell's level.

description.p: You have found a way to do more with less. As an action, for 3 Cursed Energy you can cause the effects of Flower Field on a single creature.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, apply Flower Field effects to a single target.

description.p: Your power has increased and been honed. As an action, for 5 Cursed Energy you give yourself the ability to drain Cursed Energy for 1 minute. On a hit with a melee attack or if you damage a creature with your Innate Technique, they lose 1 cursed energy which you gain.

sheet.description: For 5 CE, for 1 minute on a hit with a melee attack or if you damage a creature with your Innate Technique, they lose 1 cursed energy which you gain.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Vermin of Disaster

description.p: As part of the bonus action you take to summon or command your Insect Swarms, you can spend 2 cursed energy to reinforce one of your Insect Swarms with cursed energy for 1 minute. While reinforced, their size category increases by one and they deal an additional 2 damage dice on a successful Bites attack (only +1 if bloodied). If you use multiple Reinforced Insect Swarms to form a Vermin Swarm, it only gains these benefits once.

sheet.description: As part of the bonus action you take to summon or command your Insect Swarms, you can spend 2 CE to give one Insect Swarm reinforcement for 1 minute. While reinforced, their size category increases by one and they deal an additional 2 damage dice on a successful Bites attack (only +1 if bloodied). If you use multiple Reinforced Insect Swarms to form a Vermin Swarm, it only gains these benefits once.

description.p: You have learned to better control the combined actions of your Vermin Swarms, allowing you to learn the following ways to use your Swarms:

ul.li: Insect Cloak. While you are occupying the same space as one of your Vermin Swarms, you can treat the Swarm's hit points as temporary hit points for yourself.

Grasping Hand. You can spend 3 CE to replace one of your attacks on your turn with a grapple check through one of your Insect Swarms against a creature within 5 ft of the swarm. Instead of an Athletics check, you make a Cursed Energy (Arcana) check. The grapple gains a +1 bonus and +5 ft of range per size category above Medium the swarm is. On a success, the creature is grappled and can be pulled into the Insect Swarm.

Transport Via Bugs. As an action for 6 CE, you form a link between one Large or larger Vermin Swarm within 10 ft and connect it to another Large or larger Vermin Swarm at any distance. Before the end of your turn, you can spend 5 ft of movement to walk into the space of the Insect Swarm within 5 ft, reappearing within 5 ft of the destination Insect Swarm.

description.p: While you are occupying the same space as one of your Vermin Swarms, you can treat the Swarm's hit points as temporary hit points for yourself.

sheet.description: While you are occupying the same space as one of your Vermin Swarms, you can treat the Swarm's hit points as temporary hit points for yourself.

description.p: You can spend 3 CE to replace one of your attacks on your turn with a grapple check through one of your Insect Swarms against a creature within 5 ft of the swarm. Instead of an Athletics check, you make a Cursed Energy (Arcana) check. The grapple gains a +1 bonus and +5 ft of range per size category above Medium the swarm is. On a success, the creature is grappled and can be pulled into the Insect Swarm.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, replace one attack with a grapple check through one of your Insect Swarms against a creature within 5 ft of the swarm. Instead of an Athletics check, make a CE Arcana check. The grapple gains a +1 bonus and +5 ft of range per size category above Medium the swarm is. On a success, the creature is grappled and can be pulled into the Insect Swarm.

description.p: You can use your Insect Swarms to transport you and your allies. As a bonus action, you can command a swarm to carry you or another creature within 5 ft of the swarm. The swarm can move up to its speed while carrying the load.

sheet.description: Command an Insect Swarm to carry you or another creature within 5 ft of the swarm. The swarm can move up to its speed while carrying the load.

description.p: As a bonus action for 6 CE, you transform a weapon you are holding. It loses all previous benefits and becomes the Special Grade Cursed Tool: Festering Life Sword. You can only have one weapon transformed this way at a time and this tool disappears when you finish a long rest or die.

sheet.description: For 6 CE, transform a weapon you are holding into a Festering Life Sword. It loses all previous benefits. You can only have one weapon transformed this way at a time and this tool disappears when you finish a long rest or die.

description.p: Through much training, you gain the following martial options:

ul.li: Takedown. After a melee hit, grapple or shove as a bonus action.
Judo Throw. On hit vs grappled target: Strength save or prone; fail by 5+ = stunned.
Flying Drop Kick. Action: jump and make an unarmed attack, dealing damage and pushing 30 ft.

sheet.description: You can use Takedown, Judo Throw, and Flying Drop Kick as Martial Prowess options.

description.p: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack on your turn, you can try to grapple or shove the target as a bonus action.

sheet.description: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack on your turn, you can try to grapple or shove the target as a bonus action.

description.p: When you hit a grappled creature with a melee attack, you can grab the target and flip them on their back. If the target is Huge or smaller, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you knock them prone, and if they fail it by 5 or more they are stunned until the end of their next turn.

sheet.description: When you hit a grappled creature with a melee attack, you can force them to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you knock them prone, and if they fail it by 5 or more they are stunned until the end of their next turn.

description.p: You can use an action to jump up and land a Flying Drop Kick. You target a creature within your jump distance with an unarmed attack (Calculator here). The target takes your unarmed damage, and is pushed back 30ft.

sheet.description: Perform a unarmed attack against a creature within your jump distance. They are pushed back 30ft.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Extreme Divergent Flow

description.p: You take full advantage of the unpredictability of your attacks. After a Divergent Flow is landed, you can force the enemy to make a Cursed Energy check against your DC. On a failure, you notice their distraction and can use your reaction to make an attack against them at advantage.

sheet.description: After a Divergent Flow is landed, you can force the enemy to make a Cursed Energy check against your DC. On a failure, you can use your reaction to make an attack against them at advantage.

element.name: Maximum: Soul Punch
element.prerequisite: Must have Chosen by the Sparks

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Chosen by the Sparks

description.p: You have mastered your ability to target the soul. Before you make an unarmed strike, you can choose to spend 5 CE to cause any unarmed strikes you make on your turn to target the soul of a creature you attack. These attacks' damage reduces the target's maximum hit point and cannot be reduced or healed with Cursed Techniques or Reverse Cursed Technique as it targets the soul instead of the body. On a hit, the creature's output drops by 1, causing them to be able to spend 1 less cursed energy for cursed techniques. You can reduce a creature's output up to your Cursed Energy modifier. This effect lasts until the target takes a short or long rest. A creature whose output has been reduced cannot use maximum output techniques.

sheet.description: Before you make an unarmed strike, you can choose to spend 5 CE to cause any unarmed strikes you make on your turn to target the soul of a creature. Your attacks reduce the target's maximum hit points and cannot be healed with Cursed Techniques or Reverse Cursed Technique and reduce the amount of CE they can spend for cursed techniques by 1. You can reduce a creature's output up to {{ce:modifier}}. This effect lasts until the target takes a short or long rest. A creature whose output has been reduced cannot use maximum output techniques.

description.p: You have learned to increase the density of your stone creations. The amount of hit points each cube created by your Excavation now equals 10 times your CE modifier. Stone constructs created by your other Innate Technique features gain an additional 10 hit points.

As a reaction, you can cause one of your stone constructs within 60 feet of you to explode. Any creature within 5 feet must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take 1d12 bludgeoning damage and are knocked prone. You can spend CE up to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) as part of this reaction to heighten the explosion and deal an additional 1d12 bludgeoning damage per CE and cause creatures in range to take half as much damage on a success.

sheet.description: Cause one of your stone constructs within 60 feet of you to explode. Any creature within 5 feet must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take 1d12 bludgeoning damage and are knocked prone. You can spend up to {{proficiency:half}} CE to heighten the explosion and deal an additional 1d12 bludgeoning damage per CE and cause creatures in range to take half as much damage on a success.

description.p: You are no longer required to maintain concentration to maintain your Stone Arms. Additionally, while spending 1 CE each turn to maintain your hands, you are able to control your Stone Arms as if casting the spell Bigby's Hand. A creature that gains the restrained condition from your Stone Arms has their movement speed halved for 1 minute or until they regain hit points.

sheet.description: Your Stone Arms do not need concentration. While maintaining your hands, you are able to control your Stone Arms as if casting the spell Bigby's Hand. A creature that gains the restrained condition from your Stone Arms has their movement speed halved for 1 minute or until they regain hit points.

description.p: You have learned to minimize the damage you take when using your technique. You decrease the hit point loss from using any part of your body as an explosive by an amount equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1 hit point).
At 6th level, this reduction increases to an amount equal to twice your Constitution modifier plus your level (minimum 1 hit point).

sheet.description: Decrease the hp loss from using body parts by {{explosive:flesh:reduced:harm}} to a minimum of 1.

description.p: You have learned how to create different types of explosions, making them create different effects. You can spend additional cursed energy equal to the energy spent in using the bomb to make them an elemental bomb. The elemental bomb will deal additional dice of fire, cold or lightning damage, equal to the original bombs' damage.

sheet.description: You can spend additional CE equal to the energy spent in using a explosive to make it do additional dice of fire, cold, or lightning damage equal to the original bombs' damage.

description.p: Your explosive prowess has reached new heights, allowing you to trigger massive blasts from your removed body parts. As an action, you can spend cursed energy equal to the body part to trigger a delayed explosion. Choose a body part you have removed within 30 ft. It detonates at the end of your next turn, forcing all creatures within 20 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, they take Xd12 force damage and are knocked prone. On a success, they take half damage and are not knocked prone. You can spend additional cursed energy up to half your proficiency bonus (rounded up) to increase the damage by an additional Xd12 for every cursed energy spent. The X is your Cursed Energy modifier.

sheet.description: You can spend CE equal to a body part you have removed within 30 ft to detonate it at the end of your next turn, forcing all creatures within 20 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, they take {{ce:modifier}}d12 force damage and are knocked prone. On a success, they take half damage and are not knocked prone. You can spend up to {{proficiency:half:up}} additional CE up to increase the damage by an additional {{ce:modifier}}d12 for every cursed energy spent.

description.p: You or the DM chooses one creature you touch as your Destined One. You can always tell where your Destined One is as the thread of Fate always guides your hand. While within 30 ft of your Destined One, you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened. Additionally, once per round you can add your proficiency bonus to your AC against an attack roll made against you or to a saving throw you make, potentially changing the outcome. You cannot change the creature chosen as your Destined One unless they die.

sheet.description: You or the DM chooses one creature you touch as your Destined One. You can always tell where your Destined One is. While within 30 ft of your Destined One, you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened. Once per round you can add {{proficiency}} to your AC against an attack roll made against you or to a saving throw you make. You cannot change the creature chosen as your Destined One unless they die.

element.name: Web of Fate
element.prerequisite: Must have Cursed Eyes of Fate

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Cursed Eyes of Fate

description.p: When you cast a Fate onto targets, you can instead target a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus that are within range. If the Fate requires Touch, you must touch all targets of the technique.

sheet.description: When you cast a Fate onto targets, you can instead target {{proficiency}} creatures that are within range. If the Fate requires Touch, you must touch all targets of the technique.

element.name: Maximum: Shears of Three Fates
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Road of Karma

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Road of Karma

description.p: As an action for 15 cursed energy, you form a scissor with your fingers and snip one of the threads of fate of a creature you can see within 5 ft of you. At the start of each of the target's turns, they must make a Death save. This roll cannot have advantage or disadvantage and the target gains 1 level of exhaustion for every failed death save. If the target fails 3 death saves, they fall to 0 hit points and die. They are unable to be revived by cursed techniques. If the target succeeds 3 death saves or you are reduced to 0 hit points or die, the technique ends and the target becomes immune to this technique for 3 days.

sheet.description: For 15 CE, force a creature within 5 ft of you that you can see to start making Death saves. This roll cannot have advantage or disadvantage and the target gains 1 level of exhaustion for every failed death save. If the target fails 3 death saves, they fall to 0 hit points and die. They are unable to be revived by cursed techniques. If the target succeeds 3 death saves or you are reduced to 0 hit points or die, the technique ends and the target becomes immune to this technique for 3 days.

description.p: Your foresight ability has reached even greater potential, reaching the point some consider enlightened. Whenever you have advantage because of your technique, you can roll 3 dice instead of 2 and keep the highest roll. Also, whenever a creature has disadvantage on attack rolls against you because of your technique, they roll 3 dice instead of 2 and keep the lowest roll.

sheet.description: Whenever you have advantage because of your technique, you can roll 3 dice instead of 2 and keep the highest roll. Also, whenever a creature has disadvantage on attack rolls against you because of your technique, they roll 3 dice instead of 2 and keep the lowest roll.

description.p: You have created a more powerful version of your G Warstaff, being a true weapon of destruction. As a bonus action while you have G Warstaff active, you can spend 4 cursed energy to evolve it into its true form, GG True Warstaff. It gains the following benefits for 1 minute:

ul.li: It deals one additional damage dice
Its reach is 15ft instead of 10ft
When you score a critical hit against a creature, you gain 2 seconds instead of 1
It gains a +1 to critical range

sheet.description: For 4 CE while your Warstaff is active, it deals one additional damage dice, its reach is 15ft, when you score a critical hit you gain 2 seconds instead of 1, and it gains a +1 to critical range.

element.name: Maximum: Omniscient Reader's Viewpoint
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Storyboard Dominion

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Storyboard Dominion

description.p: You have gained the ability to not only see, but also manipulate the contents of your manga panel. At the beginning of your turn, you can choose a creature that you can see that is marked by 6 seconds and roll a d20. Record the roll. As a reaction, you can replace one roll that creature makes with the result of that roll. At the beginning of your turn, you lose any unused rolls.

sheet.description: At the beginning of your turn, you can choose a creature that you can see that is marked by 6 seconds and roll a d20. Record the roll. As a reaction, you can replace one roll that creature makes with the result of that roll. At the beginning of your turn, you lose any unused rolls.

description.p: You have trained with your hair and unlocked the following maneuvers:
Barbed Defense. As a reaction to being targeted by an attack, you can make a Scorpion Hair attack against the attacker if they are within range.
Hair Wrapping. On a hit with your Scorpion Hair, you can choose to deal no damage and instead grapple the creature. You can use your Cursed Energy modifier to make grapple checks with your hair.
Split Ends. Once per turn, when you hit with your Scorpion Hair, you can add your Cursed Energy modifier to the damage roll.
As a reaction to being targeted by an attack, you can make a Scorpion Hair attack against the attacker if they are within range.

sheet.description: As a reaction to being targeted by an attack, you can make a Scorpion Hair attack against the attacker if they are within range.

description.p: On a hit with your Scorpion Hair, you can choose to deal no damage and instead grapple the creature. You can use your Cursed Energy modifier to make grapple checks with your hair.

sheet.description: On a hit with your Scorpion Hair, you can choose to deal no damage and instead grapple the creature. You can use your CE modifier to make grapple checks with your hair.

description.p: Once per turn, when you hit with your Scorpion Hair, you can add your Cursed Energy modifier to the damage roll.

sheet.description: Once per turn, when you hit with your Scorpion Hair, you can add {{ce:modifier}} to the damage roll.

description.p: You can now use more cursed energy to imbue one Mega-drill. As an action for 4 cursed energy, you can shoot this Mega-drill in a 5 ft wide, 80 ft long line. Creatures in this line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it deals 6d10 piercing damage. On a success, they take half the damage. This technique deals maximum damage to objects and structures and ignores half and three quarters cover. The damage of this technique increases to 8d10 at 6th level and becomes 10d10 at 11th level.

sheet.description: For 4 CE, creatures in a 5 ft wide, 80 ft long line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take {{hair:manipulation:mega:drill:damage}}d10 piercing damage. On a success, they take half the damage. This technique deals maximum damage to objects and structures and ignores half and three quarters cover.

description.p: You have learned the following destructive ways to amplify your Aircraft Hair forms:

Copter Frenzy. As an action for 5 CE, you force all creatures within 15 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save they take your Helicopter Hair damage. On a successful save, they take half as much.

Jet Crash. The damage a creature takes from impacting a solid surface from your crash increases to 1d6 per 10 ft traveled in a straight line before hitting the. This becomes 1d8 at 11th level and 1d10 at 18th level.

sheet.description: If using Helicopter Hair, as an action for 5 CE force all creatures within 15 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw taking your Helicopter Hair damage on a fail and half as much on a save. If using Jet Hair, the damage a creature takes from impacting a solid surface from your crash increases to 1d{{hair:manipulation:air:bomber:damage}}.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Hands of Love

description.p: You have mastered the basic use of your love hands. Whenever you Shove a creature, they are instead pushed 10 feet. When creatures are grappled or restrained, they take a d6 bludgeoning damage at the start of their turn. Structures also take maximum damage from your Love Hands.

sheet.description: Whenever you Shove a creature, they are instead pushed 10 feet. When creatures are grappled or restrained, they take a d6 bludgeoning damage at the start of their turn. Structures also take maximum damage from your Love Hands.

description.p: You use your Love Hands in new and innovative ways. As an action, you can make your Hands into a solid platform. This can be used for traversing, or area control and gives you and another creature that is large or smaller a hover speed of 15 ft while on it.

sheet.description: Make your Hands into a solid platform. This gives you and another creature that is large or smaller a hover speed of 15 ft while on it.

description.p: As an action for 8 CE, you can choose to instantly put a creature you can see under the effects of Cutie Honey without needing to restrain them.

sheet.description: For 8 CE, instantly put a creature you can see under the effects of Cutie Honey without needing to restrain them.

description.p: You have gained the ability to further shape your ice into a weapon. As an action for 1 Cursed Energy you can make a weapon made of ice or transform ice into a weapon for 1 minute. Once per turn, you can deal additional cold damage equal to your CE modifier on an attack made with that weapon.

sheet.description: For 1 CE, you can make a ice weaponn for 1 minute. Once per turn, you can deal {{ce:modifier}} additional cold damage on an attack made with that weapon.

description.p: The cold of your ice has increased exponentially. While restrained by your ice, your opponent gains an additional temporary level of exhaustion and a -2 to attack rolls and ability checks.

sheet.description: While restrained by your ice, your opponent gains an additional temporary level of exhaustion and a -2 to attack rolls and ability checks.

description.p: You now freeze through flesh and muscle. As an action while within 5 ft of a creature that is restrained by your ice, you can break off a limb that is not the head or torso (Using the limb damage table). The creature is then no longer restrained.

sheet.description: While within 5 ft of a creature that is restrained by your ice, you can break off a limb that is not the head or torso (Using the limb damage table). The creature is then no longer restrained.

description.p: You have mastered your observation of the soul and learned grotesque molds to shape them into. You gain the two of the following ways to use transfigured humans (this feat can be repeated):

Remote Transfiguration. When using Transfiguration, you can instead imprint a small, latent amount of cursed energy upon their souls, allowing you to transfigure them from large distances (only one touch per creature).

Wall of Flesh. As an action, you spend humans up to your proficiency bonus to form a flat panel of human flesh up to 5 ft long and wide per human used in an unoccupied space within 5 ft. The wall's AC equals your CE save DC and has hit points equal to 5 x the combined CR of the creatures used. You can move it 5 ft as a bonus action and can have only one wall at a time.

Human Weapon. As a bonus action, you form a creature into a simple or martial weapon or ammunition. The weapon gains a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls if the creature's CR is 5 or higher and increases by +1 per 5 CR to a maximum of +4.

People Spikes. As an action, you spend humans up to your proficiency bonus, covering a 5 ft area per human on the ground within 30 ft of you with human spikes. A creature that walks through this area takes 2d8 piercing damage.
You have mastered your observation of the soul and learned grotesque molds to shape them into. You gain the two of the following ways to use transfigured humans (this feat can be repeated):

Remote Transfiguration. When using Transfiguration, you can instead imprint a small, latent amount of cursed energy upon their souls, allowing you to transfigure them from large distances (only one touch per creature).

Wall of Flesh. As an action, you spend humans up to your proficiency bonus to form a flat panel of human flesh up to 5 ft long and wide per human used in an unoccupied space within 5 ft. The wall's AC equals your CE save DC and has hit points equal to 5 x the combined CR of the creatures used. You can move it 5 ft as a bonus action and can have only one wall at a time.

Human Weapon. As a bonus action, you form a creature into a simple or martial weapon or ammunition. The weapon gains a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls if the creature's CR is 5 or higher and increases by +1 per 5 CR to a maximum of +4.

People Spikes. As an action, you spend humans up to your proficiency bonus, covering a 5 ft area per human on the ground within 30 ft of you with human spikes. A creature that walks through this area takes 2d8 piercing damage.
When using Transfiguration, you can instead imprint a small, latent amount of cursed energy upon their souls, allowing you to transfigure them from large distances (only one touch per creature).

sheet.description: When using Transfiguration, you can instead imprint latent CE, allowing you to transfigure them from large distances (only one touch per creature).

description.p: As an action, you spend humans up to your proficiency bonus to form a flat panel of human flesh up to 5 ft long and wide per human used in an unoccupied space within 5 ft. The wall's AC equals your CE save DC and has hit points equal to 5 x the combined CR of the creatures used. You can move it 5 ft as a bonus action and can have only one wall at a time.

sheet.description: Spend up to {{proficiency}} humans to make a 5 ft long and wide wall per human used in an unoccupied space within 5 ft. The wall has {{ce:dc:total}} AC and hit points equal to 5 x the combined CR of the creatures used. You can move it 5 ft as a bonus action and can have only one wall at a time.

description.p: As a bonus action, you form a creature into a simple or martial weapon or ammunition. The weapon gains a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls if the creature's CR is 5 or higher and increases by +1 per 5 CR to a maximum of +4.

sheet.description: Form a creature into a simple or martial weapon or ammunition. The weapon gains a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls if the creature's CR is 5 or higher and increases by +1 per 5 CR to a maximum of +4.

description.p: As an action, you spend humans up to your proficiency bonus, covering a 5 ft area per human on the ground within 30 ft of you with human spikes. A creature that walks through this area takes 2d8 piercing damage.

sheet.description: Spend up to {{proficiency}} humans covering a 5 ft area per human on the ground within 30 ft of you with human spikes. A creature that walks through this area takes 2d8 piercing damage.

description.p: When you use Ally-Alteration on a Non-Sorcerer, you can instead spend 10 cursed energy to awaken the sorcerer, making a DC 25 Cursed Energy check to awaken the target. On a success, their potential grows over the course of 1d4 + 4 days, gaining a level in a Jujutsu Sorcerer class of the DM's choice. Roll a d100 to see if the target has an Innate Technique despite being a Non-Sorcerer. On a 99-100, the target has an innate technique.
If you roll below a 15 on the Cursed Energy check, the target takes your Transfiguration damage, potentially making the target into a transfigured human.

sheet.description: When you use Ally-Alteration on a Non-Sorcerer, you can instead spend 10 CE to make a DC 25 CE check. If you roll below a 15, the target takes your Transfiguration damage, potentially making the target into a transfigured human. On a success, their potential grows over the course of 1d4 + 4 days, gaining a level in a Jujutsu Sorcerer class of the DM's choice. Roll a d100 and on a 99-100, the target has an innate technique.

description.p: Your manipulation over your soul has increased dramatically, making you almost impossible to kill by those who cannot target your soul. When you use Self-Alteration, you can instead spend cursed energy up to your Cursed Energy modifier to regain 5 hit points per cursed energy spent. You can regrow a limb for every 2 cursed energy spent in this way. At 11th level, this instead becomes 10 hit points per CE, can be used as a reaction to damage, and costs 1 CE to regrow limbs.

When you are reduced to 0 hit points while you have at least 1 CE, by a method that does not directly harm your soul, you are instead incapacitated until you regain hit points and do not make death saves. While incapacitated in this way, the only action you can take is to use Soul Alteration as an action.

sheet.description: When you use Self-Alteration, you can instead spend up to {{ce:modifier}} CE to regain 5 hit points per CE spent. You can regrow a limb for every 2 CE spent in this way. When you are reduced to 0 hit points while you have at least 1 CE by a method that does not directly harm your soul, you are instead incapacitated until you regain hit points and do not make death saves. While incapacitated in this way, you can only use Soul Alteration as an action.

When you use Self-Alteration or as a reaction to damage, you can instead spend up to {{ce:modifier}} CE to regain 10 hit points per CE spent. You can regrow a limb for every 1 CE spent in this way. When you are reduced to 0 hit points while you have at least 1 CE by a method that does not directly harm your soul, you are instead incapacitated until you regain hit points and do not make death saves. While incapacitated in this way, you can only use Soul Alteration as an action.

element.name: Maximum: Instant Spirit Body of Distorted Killing
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Self Embodiment of Perfection

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Self Embodiment of Perfection

description.p: As an action for 8 cursed energy, you mold your body and soul into a form most befitting you. A form suited for killing. For 1 hour, your AC cannot be lower than 25 and you gain damage reduction equal to your CE modifier as your body has become impossibly hard and dense. Additionally, you gain elbow blades that count as simple melee weapons that you are proficient with. They deal 1d12 + your Cursed Energy modifier in piercing damage on a hit. When you make an attack with your Elbow Blades, you can spend 1 cursed energy to gain advantage on and increase the range of the attack to 15 feet as the Elbow Blade extends and shoots out at the target.

sheet.description: For 8 CE, for 1 hour your AC cannot be lower than 25 and you gain {{ce:modifier}} damage reduction. Additionally, you gain elbow blades that count as simple melee weapons that you are proficient with which deal 1d12 + {{ce:modifier}} piercing damage on a hit. When you make an attack with your Elbow Blades, you can spend 1 CE to gain advantage and increase the range of the attack to 15 feet.

description.p: You have learned to add a bit of humor to your technique. When a cursed creature sees a distorted face, you can spend 2 CE to force the Intelligence saving throw to be made at disadvantage by making the face look extra goofy. Additionally, you can spend 1 CE to change the distortion, causing them to have to make an additional Intelligence save on their next turn if they see a distorted face.

sheet.description: When a cursed creature sees a distorted face, you can spend 2 CE to force the Intelligence saving throw to be made at disadvantage. Additionally, you can spend 1 CE to have them make an additional Intelligence save on their next turn if they see a distorted face.

description.p: You have begun to work on your tracking skills over your illusions so you can track your targets at all times.

When someone is cursed by your technique you can spend an additional 3 cursed energy to forgo the effects of the illusions but enhance the tracking. Somewhere on the target's body, a mark appears out of sight without their knowledge. They can make an Investigation check against your Cursed Energy DC to become aware of the curse or if someone else points it out. This mark will last for 24 hours and while it's active you will always have a precise location of the target within a range of 300 ft + your proficiency bonus x 100 ft. At the beginning of each day you can spend 5 Cursed Energy to keep it active for another 24 hours.
If a target leaves your range, you cannot track them until they reenter your range. You cannot extend your tracking period if they are not within range.

sheet.description: For 3 additional CE, you can forgo the effects of your illusions to place a mark on a cursed creature's body out of sight without their knowledge. They can make an Investigation check against your Cursed Energy DC to become aware of the curse or if someone else points it out. This mark lasts for 24 hours and while it's active you will always have a precise location of the target within a range of {{cursed:tracking:range}}. At the beginning of each day you can spend 5 CE to keep it active for another 24 hours. If a target leaves your range, you cannot track them until they reenter your range. You cannot extend your tracking period if they are not within range.

description.p: For 10 cursed energy, you can force a creature in range to make a Cursed Energy saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, you completely fool all of their senses for 1 minute. You can freely create illusions around the target as if they are under your Cursed Illusion or Illusive Terrain. They automatically fail checks to determine the illusions are fake as their mind rationalizes any inconsistencies.
