Additionally, the target believes so strongly that your illusions are real that they can actually take damage. Once per turn if you create a harmful illusion, the target can take psychic damage equal to your proficiency bonus in d6s.

sheet.description: For 10 CE, you can force a creature in range to make a CE saving throw at disadvantage. On a failure, for 1 minute you can freely create illusions around the target as if they are under your Cursed Illusion or Illusive Terrain. They automatically fail checks to determine the illusions are fake. Once per turn if you create a harmful illusion, the target takes {{proficiency}}d6 psychic damage.

description.p: You have fully mastered your Inverse technique, allowing you to withstand devastating attacks while weak strikes take a heavy toll. When you take damage, the effect depends on whether it exceeds or falls below your threshold.

If the damage exceeds your threshold, you reduce it by an amount equal to your level. However, if the damage is below your threshold, you increase the damage taken by an amount equal to your level. This makes you near-invulnerable to powerful blows, but even the lightest strike can prove perilous.

sheet.description: When you take damage, if it exceeds your threshold reduce it by an additional {{level}} and if it is below increase it by an additional {{level}}.

description.p: While training on your technique, you have found out you have a lot more to offer than just being an indestructible powerhouse. You have learned how to alter a whole area to be affected by your technique. As a bonus action for 6 cursed energy, you cause all objects and creatures within 30 ft of you to be affected by your Inverse technique for 1 minute. A creature or object is no longer affected by Inverse if they leave the range of the field. You can deactivate this as a bonus action.

sheet.description: For 6 CE, all objects and creatures within 30 ft of you are affected by your Inverse technique for 1 minute. A creature or object is no longer affected by Inverse if they leave the range of the field. You can deactivate this as a bonus action.

description.p: You've learned to channel your inverted energy outward, inverting the flow of harm itself while your Inverse is active:

ul.li: When you take damage within your threshold, you can spend 4 CE as a reaction to reflect half of that damage back to the attacker (damage becomes force or necrotic, your choice) if they are within 30 ft.

When you take damage below your threshold, you can instead spend 1 CE as a free action to transfer the pain. Choose a creature within 30 ft to take force or necrotic damage (your choice) equal to twice your CE modifier.

sheet.description: While Inverse is active, when you take damage above your threshold, you can spend 4 CE as a reaction to reflect half of that damage back to the attacker as necrotic or force damage if they are within 30 ft. You may also spend 1 CE as a free action when you take damage below your threshold to choose a creature within 30 ft to take {{inverse:antithesis:damage}} necrotic or force damage.

description.p: Through intense training and familiarizing yourself with your technique, you have gained the ability to apply Lapse Blue to your fists. Once per turn, when you make an unarmed strike, by spending cursed energy you can make them suffer the effects of Lapse Blue on a hit. For this attack you can use your cursed energy modifier for attack rolls. When you use Lapse Blue in this way, the target takes additional damage equal to Lapse Blue on a hit and you gain advantage on that attack roll as your technique attracts the target.

If you have access to Reversal Red, you can also imbue Red to your fists. When you use Red in this way, the target takes additional damage equal to Reversal Red on a hit and must make a Strength saving throw or be launched the same distance as a normal Red.

sheet.description: Once per turn when you make an unarmed strike, you can apply Lapse Blue spending up to its limit in CE. You can use your CE modifier for this attack roll and have advantage on the attack. On a hit, the target takes the Lapse Blue damage. You can do the same with Reversal Red, being able to use your CE modifier for this attack roll. On a hit, the target takes the Reversal Red damage and must make a Strength saving throw or be launched the same distance as a normal Red.

element.name: Maximum: Hollow Purple
element.prerequisite: Must have Lapse Blue and Reversal Red

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Lapse Blue and Reversal Red

description.p: You have learned how to mix positive and negative space, creating imaginary mass. After using your action to prepare the technique, you can release it as an action for 10 Cursed Energy. Every creature in a 5 feet wide 100-foot line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 20d12 force damage, are moved to the end of the line, gain 1 level of exhaustion and you can target a limb from the Limb Damage Table. On a successful save, they take half as much damage.

sheet.description: For 10 CE, prepare a Hollow Purple. As an later action, every creature in a 5 feet wide 100-foot line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take 20d12 force damage, are moved to the end of the line, gain 1 level of exhaustion and you can target a limb from the Limb Damage Table. On a successful save, they take half as much damage.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Maximum: Hollow Purple

description.p: You have found a new more powerful way to fire Purple. As a reaction or action when a Blue is active, you can shoot a Red into it if it is within range. This forcefully creates a Hollow Purple that affects everything in a 100 foot radius around the source. This Hollow Purple deals maximum damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. You must also make a saving throw if you are within range. This technique cannot be used at Maximum Output.

sheet.description: When a Blue is active, you can shoot a Red into it if it is within range. This creates a Hollow Purple that deals maximum damage on a failed save or half as much on a success to everything in a 100 ft radius around the Blue. This technique cannot be used at Maximum Output.

description.p: Your reaction speed has further increased. At the cost of one CE, you gain the ability to use the reactions from effective markings for other purposes.

sheet.description: For 1 CE, you can use the reactions from Effective Marking for other purposes.

description.p: You have gained further variety with your Marks and the forces within it. As a bonus action for up to 3 CE you can force marked creatures to be repelled from the creature with the highest amount of CE. How far depends on how much CE you use, with them moving 10 feet for every CE. They take 2d8 bludgeoning damage if the repelled movement forces them into a structure, object, or creature.

sheet.description: For up to 3 CE, force marked creatures to be repelled from the creature with the highest amount of CE moving 10 feet for every CE spent. They take 2d8 bludgeoning damage if the repelled movement forces them into a structure, object, or creature.

description.p: As an action for 10 CE, you push yourself and mark a creature you touch with all your power. Marking a creature this way, attracts one body into itself causing massive damage. When you activate this, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 25d4 force damage and a level of exhaustion on a failed save or half as much on a success.

sheet.description: For 10 CE, touch a creature and force them to make a Constitution saving throw, taking 25d4 force damage and a level of exhaustion on a failed save or half as much on a success.

description.p: You have learned to more efficiently store up luck. You can now store an additional number of Miracles equal to your CE modifier.

When you or a creature you can see fails a death saving throw, you can spend 1 Miracle and 1 CE as a reaction. The roll is now considered a natural 20. You cannot experience a breakthrough or gain a Miracle in this way.

sheet.description: For 1 Miracle and 1 CE as a reaction to you or a creature failing a death saving throw, the roll is now considered a natural 20. You cannot experience a breakthrough or gain a Miracle in this way.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Fated for Fortune

description.p: Some have asked you: "Are you lucky because you have miracles by your side, or do you have miracles by your side because you're lucky?" The answer was obvious, you are naturally lucky and your technique merely reflects that. You roll all dice generated by your cursed technique twice, picking the result of the higher one. Additionally, you can make a miracle die roll its maximum amount once per long rest.

sheet.description: You roll all dice generated by your cursed technique twice, picking the result of the higher one. Additionally, you can make a miracle die roll its maximum amount once per long rest.

description.p: You have learned to channel your passion for battle and fighting spirit into your cursed technique. When you hit a creature with Missile Fist, all creatures within 5 ft (other than you) take thunder damage equal to your CE modifier from the shockwaves. You can use this effect a number of times each turn equal to your proficiency bonus. When you hit a target with a Missile Fist, you gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls against that target. This bonus can stack and lasts until the end of your turn.

sheet.description: When you hit a creature with Missile Fist, all creatures within 5 ft other than you take {{ce:modifier}} thunder damage. You can use this effect {{proficiency}} times each turn. When you hit a target with a Missile Fist, you gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls against that target. This bonus can stack and lasts until the end of your turn.

description.p: As a bonus action for 2 CE, you cover your hands in Missile Fists, gaining the following benefits for 1 minute.

ul.li: You gain a +2 bonus to AC while holding no weapons or shields.
You can use your CE modifier for attack and damage rolls using your unarmed strikes.
Your unarmed strikes deal your Missile Fist damage.
The range of your unarmed strikes becomes 10 ft.

sheet.description: For 2 CE, for 1 minute you gain a +2 bonus to AC while holding no weapons or shields, can use your CE modifier for attack and damage rolls using your unarmed strikes, your unarmed strikes deal your Missile Fist damage, and the range of your unarmed strikes becomes 10 ft.

description.p: As a reaction for 3 CE, when you or a creature within 30 ft would take damage, you interpose a Missile Fist, reducing the damage by your Missile Fist damage.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, when you or a creature within 30 ft would take damage you reduce it by your Missile Fist damage.

description.p: You have learned to reinforce your shikigami with cursed energy. As part of the bonus action to command Moon Dregs, you can spend cursed energy up to your Cursed Energy modifier to give Moon Dregs 5 temporary hit points per Cursed Energy spent. This increases to 10 temporary hit points per cursed energy at 11th level.

sheet.description: As part of the bonus action to command Moon Dregs, you can spend up to {{ce:modifier}} CE to give Moon Dregs {{moon:dregs:immortal:temp:hp}} temporary hit points per Cursed Energy spent.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Poisonous Cursed Energy

description.p: You have learned to refine your poisons within Moon Dregs to create powerful mixtures. You gain the following benefits:

ul.li: Moon Dregs deals an additional 1d6 poison damage on a hit. This increases to 2d6 at 6th level and 3d8 at 11th level.
A creature grappled by Moon Dregs takes 2d4 poison damage at the beginning of their turn. This increases to 2d8 poison damage at 11th level.
Poison can also be used as an enhancer. You gain 4 ability points you can use to increase Moon Dregs ability scores (max of 20).

sheet.description: Moon Dregs deals an additional 1d6 poison damage on a hit. A creature grappled by Moon Dregs takes 2d4 poison damage at the beginning of their turn. You gain 4 ability points you can use to increase Moon Dregs ability scores (max of 20).

Moon Dregs deals an additional 2d6 poison damage on a hit. A creature grappled by Moon Dregs takes 2d4 poison damage at the beginning of their turn. You gain 4 ability points you can use to increase Moon Dregs ability scores (max of 20).

Moon Dregs deals an additional 3d8 poison damage on a hit. A creature grappled by Moon Dregs takes 2d8 poison damage at the beginning of their turn. You gain 4 ability points you can use to increase Moon Dregs ability scores (max of 20).

description.p: You have finally learned to amplify your poisonous cursed energy into a crippling neurotoxin that locks down nerves. When you use Poisonous Cursed Energy, you can spend 8 additional cursed energy to enhance the poison. If a creature fails their save against Noxious Poison they are stunned until the end of their next turn and poisoned for 8 hours. On a successful save, the target is poisoned for 1 minute. A creature poisoned in this way is treated as being under 3 temporary levels of exhaustion. If a creature fails the save by 5 or more, they are paralyzed until the end of their next turn and poisoned for 24 hours.

This poison ignores cursed techniques that grant resistance, immunity, or other reductions to poison damage and the poisoned condition. This poison cannot be removed by cursed techniques or reverse cursed technique.

sheet.description: When you use Poisonous Cursed Energy, you can spend 8 additional CE to stun the creature until the end of their next turn and give them the poisoned condition for 8 hours if they fail the save. On a successful save, the target is poisoned for 1 minute. A creature poisoned in this way is treated as being under 3 temporary levels of exhaustion. If a creature fails the save by 5 or more, they are paralyzed until the end of their next turn and poisoned for 24 hours.

description.p: Your Cursed Energy has gained a defensive property. As a reaction to being hit by a melee attack, you can spend 2 CE to force the attacker to make a Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, the creature is paralyzed until the beginning of their next turn.

sheet.description: As a reaction to being hit by a melee attack, you can spend 2 CE to force the attacker to make a Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, the creature is paralyzed until the beginning of their next turn.

description.p: You have learned how to further manipulate your lightning. On a hit with a melee attack using a weapon it also gains a charge, and as a bonus action you can release a bolt of lightning between the weapon and yourself. Those caught in that bolt of lightning must make a Dexterity save, taking your Lightning Discharge damage on a failure or half as much on a success.

sheet.description: On a hit with a melee attack using a weapon it also gains a charge, and as a bonus action you can release a bolt of lightning between the weapon and yourself. Those caught in that bolt of lightning must make a Dexterity save, taking your Lightning Discharge damage on a failure or half as much on a success.

element.name: Grounded Charge
element.prerequisite: Must have Mythical Beast Amber Release

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Mythical Beast Amber Release

description.p: Through repeated usage of your cursed technique's release your body has grown used to the manifestation of phenomena. The side effects of using Mythical Beast Amber: Release are reduced by 1 stage. This causes stage 0 to have no side effects.

sheet.description: The side effects of using Mythical Beast Amber Release are reduced by 1 stage. This causes stage 0 to have no side effects.

element.name: True New Shadow Style
element.prerequisite: Must have New Shadow Style: Simple Domain

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have New Shadow Style: Simple Domain

description.p: Your Simple Domain now has additional hit points equal to twice your Cursed Energy modifier.

sheet.description: Your Simple Domain has {{true:shadow:style:hp}} additional hit points.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Batto Sword Draw

description.p: You have learned the fastest way to draw defensively. Once per round after activating Simple Domain, you can make a melee attack against someone who enters it.

sheet.description: Once per round after activating Simple Domain, you can make a melee attack against someone who enters it.

element.name: First Strike
element.prerequisite: Must have Evening Moon Sword Drawing

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Evening Moon Sword Drawing

description.p: You have honed your reaction speed to the finest edge. When you roll initiative you can activate Simple Domain or make an attack as a free action.

sheet.description: When you roll initiative you can activate Simple Domain or make an attack as a free action.

description.p: As an action for up to 6 cursed energy, you create up to 6 smaller Simple Domains on the ground within 30 ft of yourself that each cover a 5 ft space for 1 minute. Each Maculation functions as a Simple Domain with hit points equal to 5 x your proficiency bonus. While standing in a space with Maculation, you can use your reaction to either gain a +3 bonus to AC or reduce incoming damage by your weapon's damage until the beginning of your next turn. A Maculation disappears if you end your turn more than 30 ft away from one.

sheet.description: For up to 6 CE, create up to 6 smaller Simple Domains within 30 ft that each cover a 5 ft space for 1 minute. Each has {{new:shadow:style:maculation:hp}} hit points. While standing in one, you can use your reaction to gain +3 AC or reduce incoming damage by your weapon's damage until the beginning of your next turn.

description.p: Your luck seems to spread to those around you. You can use Great Luck on another creature of your choice instead of yourself.

sheet.description: You can use Great Luck on another creature of your choice instead of yourself.

element.name: Pachinko Manifest
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Idle Death Gamble

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Idle Death Gamble

description.p: Through thorough understanding of your domain, you can now manifest Pachinko Attacks outside your domain and have a 30 foot range when used this way. Whenever you use a Pachinko Attack, you add your Cursed Energy modifier to the damage rolls. Additionally, you reduce the Cursed Energy cost of your Pachinko Attacks by 1 CE. This reduces the CE cost of Green Balls to 0 CE, but cannot reduce the CE cost of other Pachinko Attacks to 0 CE (minimum of 1). These attacks do not build Expectation.

sheet.description: Pachinko Attacks can be used outside your domain with a range of 30 ft. You add {{ce:modifier}} to their damage rolls and reduce their CE cost by 1 (Green Balls to 0, others minimum 1). These attacks do not build Expectation.

description.p: You have fully understood the essence of your technique. As an action for 10 Cursed Energy, you can summon the Love Train. This is a 200 foot long, 20 feet wide Line attack in which a creature within range must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure they are launched 200 feet and take 15d6 bludgeoning damage. The train is summoned on a space of your choice within 30ft, and the opponent is launched in the opposite direction.

sheet.description: For 10 CE, summon a train on a space of your choice within 30 ft, forcing creatures within a 200 ft long, 20 ft wide line to make a Dexterity save or be launched 200 ft and take 15d6 bludgeoning damage.

description.p: A creature under the effects of Pain Killer now gains additional temporary hit points equal to 5 times your proficiency bonus.
A creature now gains the benefits of Pain Killer for 24 hours and is automatically stabilized if they are reduced to 0 hit points while under the effect.

sheet.description: A creature with Pain Killer is automatically stabilized if reduced to 0 hit points.

description.p: As a reaction to a creature within 10 feet (including yourself) falling to 0 hit points you can spend 4 CE and cause them to instead remain at 10 hit points and gain the benefits of Pain Killer.

sheet.description: When a creature within 10 ft falls to 0 hit points, you can spend 4 CE to have them remain at 10 hit points and gain the benefits of Pain Killer.

description.p: When you use Immobilizing Eyes, you can spend 1 additional CE as part of activating the technique concentrate your eyes on a specific part of the creature, also causing one of the following restrictions while you maintain your technique:

ul.li: Legs. The target's speed is halved. If the target tries to move more than their movement speed, they must make a Dexterity saving throw or fall prone.
Arms. The target gains an additional -3 penalty to attack rolls and cannot take object interactions.
Torso. You reduce the target's AC by 3.

sheet.description: When you use Immobilizing Eyes, you can spend 1 additional CE to halve the target's speed and force them to make a Dexterity saving throw or fall prone if they move more than their movement speed, give them -3 to attack rolls and prevent them from taking object interactions, or reduce their AC by 3.

description.p: As part of your bonus action while maintaining your technique on a creature, the target takes 2d8 psychic damage. This increases to 2d10 at 6th level, 4d8 at 11th level, and 6d8 at 18th level.

sheet.description: As part of your bonus action while maintaining your technique on a creature, the target takes 2d8 psychic damage.
As part of your bonus action while maintaining your technique on a creature, the target takes 2d10 psychic damage.
As part of your bonus action while maintaining your technique on a creature, the target takes 4d8 psychic damage.
As part of your bonus action while maintaining your technique on a creature, the target takes 6d8 psychic damage.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Reactive Eye Movement

description.p: Through constant exposure, your eyes have become surprisingly tough. Whenever your eyes take damage, the damage is halved. When your eyes' hit points would be reduced to 0 hit points, you can make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw for your eyes to stay at 1 hit point. If you have Reverse Cursed Technique, you automatically succeed. You can make this saving throw a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus each long rest.

sheet.description: Damage to your eyes is halved. When your eyes' hit points would be reduced to 0 hit points, you can make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw for your eyes to stay at 1 hit point. If you have Reverse Cursed Technique, you automatically succeed. You can make this saving throw {{proficiency}} times per long rest.

description.p: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack while Prayer is active, you can spend 3 CE to deal maximum damage.

sheet.description: Once per turn when you hit while Prayer is active, you can spend 3 CE to deal maximum damage.

description.p: You put a full stop to the rhythm of your enemy. As a reaction, you can spend 3 CE to make an attack miss, or spend 5 CE to make a creature fail a saving throw.

sheet.description: For 3 CE make an attack miss or for 5 CE make a creature fail a saving throw.

description.p: As an action for 3 CE, you double the tempo of your song and the battle. You treat every turn as if two turns passed.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, treat every turn as if two turns passed.

description.p: As a reaction to an attack or damaging effect, you can spend 2 cursed energy to create a number of blank animation frames to absorb any damage. Roll a number of d10s equal to 2 + your CE modifier. Any damage you take before the start of your next turn instead damages these blank frames, but you take any remaining damage.

sheet.description: For 2 CE, roll {{projection:sorcery:white:space:dr}}d10s that reduce any damage you take before the start of your next turn. You take any remaining damage.

description.p: As an action for 4 cursed energy, you create a 5 ft wide, 30 foot long line of frames in the air that you explosively break. All creatures in that line must make a Dexterity saving throw or take a number d10s in force damage equal to your CE modifier and are knocked prone. On a failed save, they take half as much damage.

sheet.description: For 4 CE, creatures in a 5 ft by 30 ft line must make a Dexterity save or take {{ce:modifier}}d10 force damage and be knocked prone. On a failed save, they take half damage.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Fastest Sorcerer Alive

description.p: When you activate your cursed energy you can force it past its limit for short periods of time. For an additional cursed energy per round, you also gain the following effects in addition to the other effects of your technique:

ul.li: Your speed is doubled
You gain advantage on Dexterity saves
Your Dexterity score increases by +2, ignoring your maximum
An additional action on each of your turns. That action can be used only to take the Attack action (one weapon attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object action.

sheet.description: For an additional CE per round while your technique is active, your speed is doubled, you have advantage on Dexterity saves, your Dexterity score increases by 2 ignoring your maximum, and you gain an additional action that can be used to Attack, Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object.

description.p: As an action, you can wear your puppet, this gives you your puppets HP as temporary, as well as traits and physical stats and also increases your AC by 2.

sheet.description: Wear a puppet gaining its HP as temporary HP, its traits and physical stats, and +2 AC.

description.p: After one long rest, you create a small puppet that can fit on the palm of your hand. This puppet holds a copy of consciousness when you created it and has enough charge for five minutes. You can have a number equal to your proficiency bonus and anything learned by one is shared to all. These can be activated as a free action or set to activate after a specific trigger.

sheet.description: After one long rest, you create a small puppet that can fit on the palm of your hand. You can create up to {{proficiency}} small puppets. This puppet holds a copy of consciousness when you created it and has enough charge for five minutes. These can be activated as a free action or set to activate after a specific trigger.

description.p: You gain the ability to make a puppet of your Ultimate Puppet. This puppet can have double the traits, however it also costs double the resources, time, and Cursed Energy to create.

sheet.description: You can create a puppet with double traits, but it costs double resources, time, and CE.

description.p: You gain a +1 bonus to your critical range.

sheet.description: You gain a +1 to your critical range.

description.p: You focus your Ratio on your opponent's weapon. When you are targeted by a weapon attack, you can spend 5 CE as a reaction to create and hit a vulnerable ratio point on their weapon, forcing them to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure the weapon breaks. If the weapon is a cursed tool, you must use a tool of equal or greater rank to break it, otherwise the attack fails. If used against an unarmed strike, it is treated as a limb damaging effect using the Limb Damage table. Roll critical damage with your weapon. If you deal the required damage, you break the limb. Either way, the target takes no damage and this feature cannot remove a limb.

sheet.description: When targeted by a weapon attack, for 5 CE force them to make a Dex save. On a failure, break the weapon. If the weapon is a cursed tool, you must use a tool of equal or greater rank to break it, otherwise the attack fails. If used against an unarmed strike, it is treated as a limb damaging effect using the Limb Damage table. Roll critical damage with your weapon. If you deal the required damage, you break the limb.

description.p: For an additional 6 Cursed Energy, when you land a critical hit using ratio your target automatically loses a limb that is not the head or torso, using the limb damage table.

sheet.description: When you land a critical hit using Ratio, you can spend 6 CE to cause the target to automatically lose a limb using the limb damage table (not head or torso).

description.p: You've learned that just playing isn't enough, you need to build up to the best part. As an action you can start playing your instrument to charge up your technique. You can charge your technique using your action this way for a number of times equal to your CE modifier while concentrating, after which you must release your technique. You can use your reaction to launch either a Sound Blast or Sound Wave at any time while playing this way and add 2 damage die per action you use to play before releasing and ignore resistances.

sheet.description: Charge up your technique. You can charge your technique {{ce:modifier}} times after which you must release your technique. You can use your reaction to launch either a Sound Blast or Sound Wave that ignore resistances at any time while playing this way and add 2 damage die per charge.

description.p: As a bonus action for 5 cursed energy, you can introduce a vibe of your choice for 1 minute while concentrating. You can only have one Vibe active at a time. Deafened creatures automatically succeed.

ul.li: Happy Vibes. Your music gives happy and upbeat vibes. On a hit with your Sound Blast, the target makes a Charisma saving throw or be Charmed and only hear you and your music until the Vibe ends. They can repeat the save at the beginning of their turn.

Sad Vibes. Your music gives sad and depressed vibes, bringing the mood down. On a hit with your Sound Blast, the target makes a Charisma saving throw or fall prone and their speed becomes 0 until the Vibe ends. They can repeat the save at the beginning of their turn.

Angry Vibes. Your music gives off angry and rebellious vibes! On a hit with your Sound Blast, the target makes a Charisma saving throw or becomes berserk until the Vibe ends. A berserk creature uses their reaction to make a melee attack at the closest creature at the start of their turn and must use their action to attack. They can repeat the save at the beginning of their turn.

sheet.description: For 5 CE, for 1 minute while concentrating, on a hit with your Sound Blast the target suffers an effect until you lose concentration if they fail a Charisma save which deafened creatures automatically suceed. They are either charmed and can only hear you, prone and their speed becomes 0, or must use thier reaction to make a melee attack at the closest creature at the start of their turn and can only use their action to attack. A creature can repeat the save at the beginning of their turn. You can only have one option active at a time.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Maximum: Epic Solo

description.p: Looks like the band is getting back together! An epic guitar solo is always welcome, but people came for a show. When an allied creature ends their turn within 15 feet of you during your Epic Solo, their next attack or technique deals additional d4 in thunder damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

sheet.description: When an allied creature ends their turn within 15 feet of you during your Epic Solo, their next attack or technique deals {{proficiency}}d4 more thunder damage.

description.p: You have learned to increase the potency of your blood's properties. When you use a Rot Technique, you can spend additional HP up to twice your CE modifier to increase the damage dice of acid dealt by your technique by one tier for every 2 HP spent (d8-d10-d12) to a max of d12.

sheet.description: When you use a Rot Technique, you can spend up to {{rot:condensed:decay:hp}} additional HP to increase the damage dice of acid dealt by your technique by one tier for every 2 HP spent to a max of d12.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Maximum: Wing King

description.p: Your blood has become bubbling and black. A creature that fails a Constitution saving throw against your technique by 5 or more gains a level of exhaustion. A creature cannot gain more than 3 levels of exhaustion from this feature.

sheet.description: A creature that fails a Constitution saving throw against your technique by 5 or more gains a level of exhaustion. A creature cannot gain more than 3 levels of exhaustion from this feature.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Maximum: Wing King

description.p: You have mastered your Maximum technique, granting you the following benefits while using Wing King:

ul.li: You gain the Evasion feature
You can now make four lines of blood attacks as a free action on your turn
You can use a Rot Technique as a bonus action

sheet.description: While using Maximum: Wing King, you can use Evasion and use a Rot Technique as a bonus action.

description.p: While some may call you morbid, you are great at finding the corpses of deceased people. You gain proficiency in Investigation or expertise if already proficient, and will gain advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) and (History) checks related to dead humanoids and their techniques.

sheet.description: You have advantage on Investigation and History checks related to dead humanoids and their techniques.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Ritual of Resurrection

description.p: You won't allow anyone to interrupt your seance. You have advantage on saving throws to maintain concentration for your Seance technique and the time to complete your Ritual is decreased to 3 rounds. Additionally, you decrease the saving throw DC a target makes against the dead humanoid's Soul Information by your CE modifier.

sheet.description: You have advantage on concentration saves for Seance, reduce Ritual of Resurrection time to 3 rounds, and decrease Soul Information's save DC by {{ce:modifier}}.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Maximum: Black Seance

description.p: When a target runs out of cursed energy and can no longer maintain the seance, they can choose to forcefully hold the spirit within their body. At the beginning of the target's turn, they instead spend 5 HP, reducing their maximum by the same amount. Your hit point maximum is restored by 15 after each long rest.

sheet.description: When a target runs out of CE in a Seance body, at the beginning of the target's turn they can instead spend 5 HP, reducing their maximum by the same amount. Their hit point maximum is restored by 15 after each long rest.

description.p: Your Dismantle attack has grown into more versatility. For an extra 1 Cursed energy you can make Dismantle into a 30 ft cone which deals critical damage to structures, creating difficult terrain and forcing a Dexterity saving throw for all creatures within range, taking Dismantle's damage on a failed save.

sheet.description: For an extra 1 CE, Dismantle becomes a 30 ft cone that forces a Dexterity save for all creatures in range, taking Dismantle's damage on a failed save. This also creates difficult terrain and does critical damage to structures.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Slicing and Severing

description.p: You have learned to further segment your attacks to target more creatures. When you spend more than 1 CE for Dismantle, you can instead fire multiple slashes splitting damage die between the slashes before attacking and making a separate attack roll for each.

sheet.description: When spending more than 1 CE on Dismantle, you can split damage dice into multiple slashes before attacking and make separate attack rolls for each one.

element.name: Maximum: World Cleave
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Malevolent Shrine

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Malevolent Shrine

description.p: You have broadened the understanding of your technique, cutting the area your target is in instead of the target itself. When you spend the maximum amount of cursed energy on Cleave, you can instead create a 5 ft wide, 10 ft long line in a point within 60 ft that you can see. Any creature in range must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure they take maximum damage, on a success they take half as much damage. This damage ignores resistances, immunities, cover, obstacles, bonuses to saving throws and any cursed techniques.

sheet.description: When you spend the maximum amount of CE on Cleave, you can instead create a 5 ft wide, 10 ft long line at a point within 60 ft that you can see. Any creature in range must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure they take maximum damage, on a success they take half as much damage. This damage ignores resistances, immunities, cover, obstacles, bonuses to saving throws and any cursed techniques.

description.p: As an action, for 3 Cursed Energy you can cast the invisibility spell as the sky surrounds you preventing others from seeing you. When you reach third level, you also gain the benefits of Sky Armor when invisible.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, you can cast invisibility
For 3 CE, you can cast invisibility. While invisible you gain Sky Armor

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Thin Ice Breaker

description.p: You can spend 6 CE to make an attack on your turn into a melee cursed energy attack roll using a melee weapon or unarmed strike. On a hit, you deal damage as normal and the target also suffers the effects of Thin Ice Breaker.

sheet.description: For 6 CE, make a attack into a melee CE attack using a melee weapon or unarmed strike. On a hit, deal damage as normal and apply Thin Ice Breaker.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Thin Ice Breaker

description.p: As an action, for 10 Cursed Energy you can spin the space around you forcing all creatures in a 30 foot radius to make a Strength saving throw or be launched 1000 feet into the air as the space rebounds upwards.

sheet.description: For 10 CE, creatures in a 30 ft radius must make a Strength save or be launched 1000 ft into the air.

description.p: You have mastered Blessing those within your Zone. Those in your Zone have their output and durability increased. When you create your Zone, all allies in range gain temporary hit points at the start of their turns equal to your Cursed Energy modifier.

sheet.description: Allies in your Zone gain {{ce:modifier}} temporary hit points at the start of their turns.

description.p: When you use Forbidden Zone, you maximize a creature's output offensively. Allies in your Zone can add a d4 to attack and damage rolls.

sheet.description: Allies in your Zone add a d4 to attack and damage rolls.

description.p: When setting up your Ritual Zone as part of your full turn action, you can spend an additional 3 cursed energy to roll a d10. Instead of you having to maintain your zone each turn, it instead lasts for a number of rounds equal to the number rolled + your Cursed Energy modifier.

sheet.description: When using Ritual Dance, you can spend an additional 3 CE to roll a d10. The Zone instead lasts that many rounds + {{ce:modifier}} without maintenance.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Minor Star Shikigami

description.p: You have learned that strengthening cursed energy lets you summon stronger shikigami. Your Minor Star Shikigami gain the following benefits.

ul.li: The Shikigami's Bite attack increases to 1d6. It becomes 1d8 at 6th level and 1d10 at 11th level.
The Shikigami gains a +2 bonus to their Dexterity score and again at 6th, and 11th level (max of 20).

When the shikigami is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

sheet.description: Your Minor Star Shikigami's Bite does 1d6, gains +2 Dex (max of 20), and when it is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

Your Minor Star Shikigami's Bite does 1d8, gains +4 Dex (max of 20), and when it is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

Your Minor Star Shikigami's Bite does 1d10, gains +6 Dex (max of 20), and when it is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Major Star Shikigami

description.p: You have learned that strengthening cursed energy lets you summon stronger shikigami. You can spend 1 additional cursed energy when you summon your Major Star Shikigami to increase its size category to Large. This increases to 2 cursed energy to make it Huge at 11th level and 4 cursed energy to make it Gargantuan at 18th level. For every size category larger than medium, it gains the following:

ul.li: +4 bonus to Strength score
+1d8 damage to Bite attacks
+30 hit points

sheet.description: You can spend 1 additional CE when you summon your Major Star Shikigami to make it Large. For every size category large than medium it gains a +4 to Strength, +1d8 to Bite, and +30 HP.

You can spend 1 additional CE when you summon your Major Star Shikigami to make it Large or 2 to make it Huge. For every size category large than medium it gains a +4 to Strength, +1d8 to Bite, and +30 HP.

You can spend 1 additional CE when you summon your Major Star Shikigami to make it Large, 2 to make it Huge, or 4 to make it Gargantuan. For every size category large than medium it gains a +4 to Strength, +1d8 to Bite, and +30 HP.

element.name: Judicial Amputation
element.prerequisite: Must have Army of the Dictator

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Army of the Dictator

description.p: If a creature fails their saving throw against your pseudo-domain by 5 or more, the damage is treated as having the Limb Removing Effect and targets one of their arms or legs.

sheet.description: Creatures that fail your pseudo-domain save by 5 or more have the damage do a limb removing effect targeting a arm or leg.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Virtual Mass Increase

description.p: After landing a critical you can choose to also push the creature 30 feet away. If they hit a solid object or structure, they take the remaining distance as fall damage.

sheet.description: On a critical hit, you can push the target 30 ft. If they hit a surface, they take remaining distance as fall damage.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Virtual Mass Improvement

description.p: When you have your mass increased, you can spend 3 Cursed Energy once per round to replace one of your attacks by kicking or throwing an object with massive strength. This creates a 5 foot wide, 60 feet long line, causing all creatures within range to make a Dexterity saving throw, taking twice your unarmed strike damage on a failed save as well as targeting a limb from the limb damage table.

sheet.description: When you have your mass increased, you can spend 3 CE once per round to replace one of your attacks with a 5 foot wide, 60 feet long line, causing all creatures within range to make a Dexterity saving throw, taking twice your unarmed strike damage on a failed save as well as targeting a limb from the limb damage table.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Virtual Mass Mastery

description.p: As an action you can sacrifice yourself, becoming a Black Hole for 1 minute, after which you die. This creates a 100 foot radius of difficult terrain where all creatures within range make a Strength saving throw at the end of your turn, being pulled 50 feet closer on a failed save or 25 feet on a success. If a creature or object gets within 5 feet of you, they are sucked into the black hole and die.

sheet.description: You become a Black Hole for 1 minute, creating a 100 ft radius of difficult terrain and forcing all creatures within range to make a Strength save at the end of your turn, being pulled 50 ft closer on a failure or 25 ft on a success. Creatures or objects within 5 ft of you die. After the minute you die.

description.p: As an action, for as much Cursed Energy as nails you unleash your nails in a cone equal to ten times the nails used you can force creatures in the area to do a Dexterity save or take a number of d6 equal to the nails used.

sheet.description: Spend any amount of CE to create a (10 ft per CE) cone which forces creatures in the area to make a Dex save or take 1d6 piercing damage per CE used.

description.p: When you use Hairpin, you can spend an additional 1 cursed energy per nail. If the creature fails its saves, it becomes Restrained by needles of cursed energy. An affected creature can make a Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check against your cursed energy save at the end of their turn, freeing themselves on a success. On a failure, they take 1d8 piercing damage.

sheet.description: When you release your nails with Hairpin, you can spend an additional 1 CE per nail making them restrain creatures if they fail the Dex save. An affected creature can make a Athletics or Acrobatics check against your CE save DC at the end of their turn, freeing themselves on a success. On a failure, they take 1d8 piercing damage.

element.name: Maximum: Last Nail in the Coffin

description.p: You have mastered the Straw Doll Technique, when you use your action to damage a straw doll with Resonance, for 8 Cursed Energy you can choose to land an Improved Resonance.

sheet.description: When using Resonance on a straw doll, you can spend 8 CE to instead apply Improved Resonance.

description.p: On a hit you can spend 2 Cursed Energy to deal an additional 2d12 radiant damage. You can increase the damage by 1d12 by spending an additional Cursed Energy. You can only spend as much cursed energy as your Cursed Energy modifier using this feature.

sheet.description: On a hit, spend 2 CE to deal additional 2d12 radiant damage. Spend up to {{ce:modifier}} extra CE to do extra 1d12 per CE spent.

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Angel's Blessing, Breakthrough

description.p: You gain a Blessing from the Dungeon Master's Guide (In Supernatural Gifts).

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Maximum: Jacob's Ladder

description.p: Through rigorous effort and control, you have managed to convince the might of Jacob's Ladder into a single blow. As an action for 8 Cursed Energy, you can make a melee Cursed Energy attack against a single creature. On a hit that creature will then suffer the full effects of Jacob's Ladder without a saving throw.

sheet.description: For 8 CE, make a melee CE attack. On a hit, the target suffers the full effects of Jacob's Ladder effects with no save.

description.p: You're grown more efficient with your teleports and have perfected their usage. The cost to use your technique instead becomes 1 CE and the cost to cast Teleport is reduced by your CE modifier.
You have learned a trick to empower your marks. You can now have a number of marks equal to five times your CE modifier. Additionally, your marks last indefinitely.

sheet.description: Your marks last indefinitely.

element.name: Maximum: Soul Swap
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Crossroads of Instance

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Crossroads of Instance

description.p: With some creative thinking you've figured out an entirely brand new way to use your technique, swapping the souls of creatures. As an action with two willing creatures within 15 feet of you, you can swap the souls of the creatures. For 1 hour or until you dismiss it at will (no action required), all of the creatures' game statistics are swapped except for their mental stats (Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma) and any non-Physical feats if they had one. However, they lose access to their innate technique features and gain access to the other's technique. They also gain the other's cursed energy, hit points, racial features, and weapon proficiencies.

When the swap ends both creatures are returned to their original bodies, reverting the changes to game statistics, but each creature can ignore one prerequisite of any feats the other creature used while in their body, except for prerequisites that require you to be or not be a certain race, prerequisites that require you to have a certain cursed technique, prerequisites that require other feats, prerequisites that require a breakthrough, or prerequisites that require you to be a certain class. A creature can only be swapped twice in a one month timeframe, after which you must wait one month before swapping that creature again. If a creature dies while swapped, the other dies once the duration ends.

sheet.description: Swap the souls of two willing creatures within 15 ft of you. For 1 hour or until you dismiss it at will, all of the creatures' game statistics are swapped except for their mental stats and any non-Physical feats. When the swap ends both creatures are returned to their original bodies, reverting the changes to game statistics, but each creature can ignore one prerequisite of any feats the other creature used while in their body except for ones that require you to be or not be a certain race, have a certain cursed technique, have other feats, have a breakthrough, or be a certain class. A creature can only be swapped twice in a one month timeframe, after which you must wait one month before swapping that creature again. If a creature dies while swapped, the other dies once the duration ends.

description.p: You have furthered your use of shadows. Choose one of the following features (This feat can be taken multiple times).

ul.li: Shadow Skulker. As an action for 2 CE you physically enter the shadows gaining full cover for a turn or until you exit from a shadow within 10 feet. At the start of your turn, you can spend 1 CE to remain in the shadow. You can hear and see through the shadow and can summon tamed shikigami within the shadow. If the shadow is sufficiently illuminated, you exit the shadow.

Shadow Ridge. As a reaction for 1 CE against a melee attack, you force a creature within 10 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure the target rolls the attack at disadvantage as their legs fall into their shadow briefly and they fall prone. You can instead spend 3 CE to give the target disadvantage on the save.
Shadow Spells. Two shadow spells below 5th level. The CE cost equals the spell's level.

description.p: You have furthered your use of shadows. Choose one of the following features (This feat can be taken multiple times).

ul.li: Shadow Skulker. As an action for 2 CE you physically enter the shadows gaining full cover for a turn or until you exit from a shadow within 10 feet. At the start of your turn, you can spend 1 CE to remain in the shadow. You can hear and see through the shadow and can summon tamed shikigami within the shadow. If the shadow is sufficiently illuminated, you exit the shadow.

Shadow Ridge. As a reaction for 1 CE against a melee attack, you force a creature within 10 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure the target rolls the attack at disadvantage as their legs fall into their shadow briefly and they fall prone. You can instead spend 3 CE to give the target disadvantage on the save.
Shadow Spells. Two shadow spells below 5th level. The CE cost equals the spell's level.

description.p: Shadow Skulker. As an action for 2 CE you physically enter the shadows gaining full cover for a turn or until you exit from a shadow within 10 feet. At the start of your turn, you can spend 1 CE to remain in the shadow. You can hear and see through the shadow and can summon tamed shikigami within the shadow. If the shadow is sufficiently illuminated, you exit the shadow.

sheet.description: For 2 CE, enter the shadows gaining full cover for a turn or until you exit from a shadow within 10 ft. At the start of your turn, you can spend 1 CE to remain in the shadow. You can hear and see through the shadow and can summon tamed shikigami within the shadow. If the shadow is sufficiently illuminated, you exit the shadow.

description.p: Shadow Ridge. As a reaction for 1 CE against a melee attack, you force a creature within 10 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure the target rolls the attack at disadvantage as their legs fall into their shadow briefly and they fall prone. You can instead spend 3 CE to give the target disadvantage on the save.

sheet.description: For 1 CE against a melee attack, force a creature within 10 ft to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure the target rolls the attack at disadvantage and they fall prone. You can instead spend 3 CE to give the target disadvantage on the save.

description.p: You have learned how to better fuse the powers of your Shadows even at the cost of creating a monstrosity. When creating a Totality, you can sacrifice a tamed shikigami even if it is not destroyed. You can use a number of shikigami up to half your proficiency bonus (rounded up) + 1 that are eligible for Totality to fuse into an eligible Non-Totality shikigami, forming a Chimera. Chimeras can have humanoid forms, but cannot be used for Well's Unknown Abyss.
You can also give an additional alteration to your altered shikigami created with Well's Unknown Abyss, counting as part of the technique.

sheet.description: Shikigamis created with Well's Unknown Abyss gain an additional alteration. Additionaly, when creating a Totality, you can sacrifice a tamed shikigami even if it is not destroyed. You can use {{ten:shadows:chimera:max}} shikigami that are eligible for Totality to fuse into an eligible Non-Totality shikigami, forming a Chimera. Chimeras can have humanoid forms, but cannot be used for Well's Unknown Abyss.

element.name: Maximum: Embodiment
element.prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Chimera Shadow Garden

p.em: Prerequisite: Must have Domain Expansion: Chimera Shadow Garden

description.p: As an action, you can spend half the cost of one of your tamed shikigami (min of 1). Rather than summoning, you instead gain their movement, actions, legendary actions, features and one ability score of your choice from the shikigami for 1 hour. (Eg. Divine Dog's movement, Max Elephant's Constitution, or Mahoraga's Adaption.) You can embody one shikigami at a time.

sheet.description: Spend half a tamed shikigami's cost (min of 1) to gain its movement, actions, features, and one ability score for 1 hour (one at a time). You can embody one shikigami at a time.

description.p: You can imbue a set of tools with your cursed energy, such as artisan's tools or thieve's tools. You can use your CE modifier for ability checks made using these tools and gain a +1 bonus for each CE spent on the imbuement up to a maximum of your CE modifier.

sheet.description: You can imbue tools to add {{ce:modifier}} for ability check made using these tools adding +1 for each CE spent up to {{ce:modifier}}.

description.p: When you are riding your Floating Tool, you gain a +5 bonus to your passive Perception and Investigation and can use your CE modifier for Dexterity saving throws.

sheet.description: While riding your Floating Tool, gain +5 to passive Perception and Investigation and can use your CE modifier for Dex saves.

description.p: As an action for 8 CE, you can imbue a cursed tool with sentience, causing it to come to life and become a Tsukumogami for 1 hour. Your Tsukumogami is always under the effects of Floating Tool and can freely move by itself. It does not count against the number of Floating Tools you can have at once, but you can only have one Tsukumogami at a time.

The Tsukumogami can take an action on your turn while you are holding it. That action can be used only to take the Attack action (one weapon attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object action. If you use Wind Scythe or Combat Tool Improvement's reaction with your Tsukumogami, you can roll three additional dice.

sheet.description: For 8 CE, animate a tool into a Tsukumogami for 1 hour which is always under the effects of Floating Tool and can freely move by itself. It does not count against the number of Floating Tools you can have at once, but you can only have one Tsukumogami at a time. It can take an Attack (one weapon attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object action on your turn while you are holding it. If you use Wind Scythe or Comvat Tool Improvement's reaction with it, you can roll 3 additional dice.

description.p: You have mastered your Unblockable Blows core option. Your blows can now treat immunity as resistance by spending 1 additional CE. You can now spend more than 1 CE in an attack.

sheet.description: For 1 additional CE, your Unblockable Blows treats immunity as resistance.

description.p: You have improved your output. When you use your Energy Blast core option with at least 4 CE, the damage dice becomes d8 and creatures must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked 10ft back and fall prone.

sheet.description: When you use Energy Blast with at least 4 CE, the damage dice becomes d8 and creatures must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked 10ft back and fall prone.

description.p: When you use your Defensive Form core option, you instead gain temporary hit points equal to four times your current CE. While you have these temporary hit points, you are resistant to Piercing, Bludgeoning, and Slashing damage.

sheet.description: When you use Defensive Form instead gain 4x your current CE in temp HP and while you have them you are resistant to Piercing, Bludgeoning, and Slashing damage.

description.p: You have hastened yourself even further. When you use your Divebomb core option, you instead increase your speed by 15 ft per CE spent and you gain a +4 bonus to AC. You deal an additional 1d6 damage for every 10 ft you move in a straight line before hitting a target with a melee attack.

sheet.description: When you use Divebomb you instead increase your speed by 15 ft per CE spent and gain a +4 bonus to AC. You deal an additional 1d6 damage for every 10 ft you move in a straight line before hitting a target with a melee attack.

description.p: Your manipulation over the manifestation of limbs and such increase. The range of your partial manifestation attacks increases to 15 ft. Additionally, when you hit with one of these attacks, you can attempt to grapple or shove the target as part of the attack.

sheet.description: Partial Manifestation attacks have 15 ft range and can grapple or shove on a hit as part of the attack.

description.p: When you copy techniques, you can keep more in the Vengeful Spirit. You can store twice as many techniques, with any additional techniques being stored within your spirit. You must use an action to retrieve them while within 5 ft of the Vengeful Spirit while it is summoned.

While your Vengeful Spirit is summoned, the number of times you can use a copied technique feature before it disappears increases by your CE modifier. The increased number of uses does not reset when your Vengeful Spirit is resummoned.

sheet.description: You can store {{ce:modifier}} more techniques, with any additional techniques being stored within your spirit. You must use an action to retrieve them while within 5 ft of the Vengeful Spirit while it is summoned. While your Vengeful Spirit is summoned, the number of times you can use a copied technique feature before it disappears increases by {{ce:modifier}}. The increased number of uses does not reset when your Vengeful Spirit is resummoned.

description.p: You and your spirit have finally made amends, making it finally pass on to the afterlife. However, the spirit left you a present before going. Your Vengeful Spirit is now fully under your control, not being berserk when you summon it. It is now a Shikigami instead of a Cursed Spirit. Although you can use your action to give it an order, making it act on its own to complete the order. While summoned, you can use your action to borrow its power, drawing cursed energy equal to your level into yourself from its pool once per day. The spirit can only be active for a number of minutes equal to your CE modifier per day.

sheet.description: Your Vengeful Spirit is now fully under your control, not being berserk when you summon it. It is now a Shikigami instead of a Cursed Spirit. You can use your action to give it an order, making it act on its own to complete the order. While summoned, you can use your action to draw {{level}} CE from its pool once per day. The spirit can only be active for {{ce:modifier}} minutes per day.
